general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonia or pulmonary infiltrates related to immune checkpoint inhibition, uncontrolled infections, active hepatitis B or C infection, known HIV infection, current pregnancy or lactation, major surgical procedure or radiotherapy within four weeks before study entry, systemic anticancer therapy within fourteen days preceding study initiation (except permitted agents), symptomatic or untreated central nervous system involvement including leptomeningeal disease or spinal cord compression, New York Heart Association class three or four heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction or cerebrovascular accident within six months, left ventricular ejection fraction < 40 % by MUGA scan or echocardiography, second primary invasive malignancy requiring active treatment or diagnosed less than two years ago (excluding adequately treated basal/squamous skin carcinoma or carcinoma in situ), concurrent anticoagulation with warfarin, recent intake of potent cytochrome P450 3A inhibitors or inducers, ingestion of grapefruit or similar citrus fruits within three days of dosing, inability to take oral medications due to gastrointestinal absorption disorders, and any serious acute or chronic medical or psychiatric illness judged by the investigators to place the participant at undue risk or compromise data integrity.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma not otherwise specified; high‑grade B‑cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 and/or BCL6 rearrangements; high‑grade B‑cell lymphoma not otherwise specified; primary mediastinal (thymic) large B‑cell lymphoma; transformed lymphoma (e.g., transformed follicular lymphoma, transformed marginal zone lymphoma, follicular lymphoma grade 3). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after ≥2 prior lines of systemic therapy, including an anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody and an anthracycline‑containing regimen; disease may have progressed after autologous stem cell transplant or patient may be ineligible/unwilling for transplant. Prior treatment required: ≥2 prior systemic therapy lines that contained an anti‑CD20 agent and an anthracycline‑containing regimen; disease must be relapsed or refractory after these lines. Prior treatment excluded: Prior CD19 or CD20 directed chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy; prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant; prior bispecific T‑cell engager antibody therapy; prior T‑cell receptor‑engineered T‑cell therapy; prior gene‑modified cellular therapy for B‑cell lymphoma. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
- Primary central nervous system lymphoma (applicable to non‑CNS cohorts)
- Richter’s transformed diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma arising from chronic lymphocytic leukaemia (outside RT cohort)
- Inability to give informed consent
- Known infection with Human Immunodeficiency Virus
- Active Hepatitis B surface antigen positivity; untreated/HBV DNA detectable
- Treated hepatitis B or C with detectable viral load; untreated hepatitis C RNA positivity
- Pharmacologically uncontrolled seizure disorders
- Autoimmune diseases affecting the central nervous system (multiple sclerosis, optic neuritis, etc.)
- Other central nervous system disorders judged to impede neurotoxicty assessment (including midline shift on MRI, elevated cerebrospinal fluid opening pressure, CSF protein >150 mg/dL)
- Recent (<3 months) whole brain radiation therapy
- Active systemic fungal, viral, or bacterial infections
- Pregnancy or breastfeeding
- Current or past malignant neoplasm excluding adequately treated basal/squamous skin carcinomas, in‑situ cervical/breast carcinoma cured ≥2 years ago, fully resected solid tumours in remission ≥2 years, hormone‑treated breast or prostate cancer in remission ≥2 years
- Severe immunocompromise from ongoing treatment of non‑neurological autoimmune conditions
- Chronic systemic glucocorticoid usage exceeding prednisolone ≈10 mg/day (up to 2 mg/day dexamethasone permissible for CNS cohort; higher doses allowable ≤7 days pre‑apheresis or post‑apheresis until lymphodepletion)
- Myocardial infarction, coronary intervention, unstable angina, or serious cardiac disease within 6 months
- Concomitant radiotherapy (except palliative normal‑tissue‑sparring radiotherapy discontinued ≥2 weeks before leukapheresis)
- Dementia interfering with assessments
- Serious acute hypersensitivity reactions to any investigational product components
- Declination to partake in long‑term lentiviral gene‑therapy follow‑up
- Prior receipt of any CAR‑T cell therapy for any indication
- Prior allogeneic stem cell transplantation for any indication
- Prior administration of bispecific T‑cell engaging antibodies for cancer
- Prior T‑cell receptor‑engineered T‑cell therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced disease with measurable lesions per RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: Receipt of platinum‑based chemotherapy with documented refractory or resistant disease. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Prior treatment with an antibody‑drug conjugate bearing a topoisomerase I payload.
• Active or progressively symptomatic central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (treated/stable CNS disease permitted).
• Persistently greater-than‑grade 1 toxicities attributable to previous systemic anticancer therapy (excluding alopecia and vitiligo).
• Clinically significant underlying lung disease requiring systemic glucocorticoids within six months.
• Current acute or chronic pancreatitis or cirrhosis (unless classified as Child‑Pugh A).
• Evidence of hepatic decompensation (clinical jaundice, encephalopathy, variceal bleeding) within sixty days.
• History of orthotropic liver transplantation.
• Prior allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.
• Uncontrolled serious cardiac illness—including recent myocardial infarction (≤six months), New York Heart Association Class III/IV heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, malignant arrhythmias, or critical valvular disease.
• Recent arterial or venous thrombotic or cerebrovascular events occurring within three months.
• Active untreated bacterial, fungal, or viral infection, including hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or Human Immunodeficiency Virus (patients with controlled chronic HBV/HCV/HIV meeting predefined virologic criteria may be enrolled).
• Documented hypersensitivity to the investigative product or excipients.
• Simultaneous enrolment in another interventional clinical investigation.
• Pregnancy or lactation.
• Prior invasive malignancy (aside from current indication) within three years, except satisfactorily treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer, non‑muscle‑invasive urothelial carcinoma, in‑situ cervix/breast/prostate cancer lacking activity for over two years.
• Any concomitant severe acute or chronic medical or psychiatric disorder judged to jeopardize participant safety or compromise data integrity.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma, hormone receptor positive, human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease or locally recurrent unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: Radiographic or clinically documented progression on a cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor–containing regimen in the metastatic setting **or** relapse/progression occurring during or within 12 months after completion of such a regimen in the adjuvant setting **and** radiographic or clinical progression on fulvestrant (alone or as part of a combination) in the metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: Receipt of more than two different chemotherapy regimens in the metastatic setting; any prior megakaryocyte extracellular signal‐regulated kinase (MEK) inhibitor; prior discontinuation of abemaciclib owing to toxicity; prior discontinuation of fulvestrant owing to toxicity. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor positive **and/or** progesterone receptor positive; Human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative (assessment per ASCO/CAP guideline). Biomarkers excluded: NA
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients with active brain metastases or carcinomatous meningitis (treated/stable brain metastases permissible); individuals with significant pre‑existing ocular diseases—including glaucoma, retinal venous occlusion, retinopathy, intra‑ocular pressure >21 mmHg, corneal epithelial instability, keratitis, or other serious ocular surface inflammation; history of rhabdomyolysis or neuromuscular disorders associated with markedly increased creatine kinase (e.g., inflammatory myopathies, muscular dystrophy, ALS, SMA); uncontrolled concurrent illnesses such as uncontrolled hypertension, uncontrolled diabetes mellitus, severe obstructive pulmonary disease, New York Heart Association class III‑IV heart failure, recent myocardial infarction (within 6 months), unstable angina, symptomatic cardiac arrhythmias, ventricular tachyarrhythmias, syncopal episodes of cardiac cause, active ischemic heart disease, recent stroke, or any life‑threatening cardiac condition; severe chronic liver disease or renal impairment outside stipulated laboratory thresholds; ongoing systemic bacterial infection requiring intravenous antibiotics, systemic fungal infection requiring intravenous antifungals, or SARS‑CoV‑2 infection within 28 days before enrolment; known allergy or hypersensitivity to VS‑6766, abemaciclib, fulvestrant, or excipients thereof; documented HIV infection with an AIDS‑defining illness within the preceding 12 months or receiving potent CYP3A4‑modulating antiretrovirals; active hepatitis B (surface antigen positive) or hepatitis C infection not adequately controlled; pregnancy or breastfeeding; inability to ingest/swallow oral medication or conditions causing marked malabsorption (gastrectomy, extensive small‑bowel resections, active inflammatory bowel disease, baseline diarrhoea grade ≥2); concomitant use of warfarin; any other serious, unmanaged medical condition (interstitial lung disease, severe resting dyspnoea requiring supplemental oxygen, uncontrolled psychiatric or neurologic disease) deeming the patient unsuitable for safe trial participation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: essential thrombocythemia. Histology allowed: Myeloid proliferative neoplasm presenting with persistent elevated platelet count (essential thrombocythemia). Cancer burden allowed: Non–high‑risk disease defined as very low, low, or intermediate risk per International Prognostic Score for Essential Thrombocytosis (absence of overt thrombosis, age \<60 with or without JAK2 V617F mutation, or age \>60 without JAK2 mutation); bone marrow/blasts ≤5%. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of high‑risk essential thrombocythemia (age \>60 with JAK2 V617F mutation and/or prior thrombosis).
High‑risk polycythemiavera (age \>60 and/or prior thrombosis).
Bone‑marrow blast proportion greater than 5%.
Concurrent enrolment in another investigational therapeutic study.
Previous splenectomy (except with principal investigator approval).
Allergic reaction to ruxolitinib or its formulation ingredients.
Use of potent cytochrome P450 3A4 inhibiting medicines.
Uncontrolled intercurrent acute illnesses.
Laboratory evidence of severe hepatic insufficiency (AST/ALT > 3× upper limit of normal) or unacceptable renal dysfunction (creatinine above institution’s ULN or estimated GFR < 60 mL/min/1.73 m², unless justified down to 30 mL/min/1.73 m²).
Active psychiatric disorders or social circumstances compromising adherence to study protocols.
Pregnancy, lactation, or lack of reliable contraception in persons of reproductive potential.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: male and female. Cancer type allowed: solid tumor (all sites). Histology allowed: any solid tumor histology. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced unresectable or distant metastatic disease with documented radiographic progression after the most recent systemic therapy and at least one meas­urable lesion per RECIST v1.1. Prior treatment required: receipt of at least one prior standard systemic anticancer therapy for the identified solid tumor (relapse or progression after standard treatment, or intolerance/to lack of standard option). Prior treatment excluded: prior HER3‑directed therapy **or** prior antibody‑drug conjugate incorporating a topoisomerase I inhibitor (unless specifically permitted by cohort‐specific amendments). clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Conventional chondrosarcoma. Histology allowed: Grade 1 or Grade 2. Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable locally advanced or metastatic disease with at least one meas­urable lesion per RECIST v1.1 and evidence of radiographic progression or recurrence. Prior treatment required: Up to one prior systemic anti‑cancer regimen in the advanced/metastatic setting (zero or one). Prior treatment excluded: Previous receipt of any IDH1 inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Documented IDH1 missense mutation (R132C/L/G/H/S) identified by central lab testing during prescreening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Unable to swallow oral medication.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Concurrent participation in another interventional clinical study.
Known symptomatic brain metastases requiring >10 mg prednisolone-equivalent steroids.
History of another primary malignancy (except cured non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, pT1‑2 prostate cancer Gleason <6, or disease‐free ≥1 year).
Major surgery within 4 weeks preceding randomisation.
Significant active cardiovascular disease within 6 months (New York Heart Association class III/IV heart failure, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, stroke).
Left ventricular ejection fraction < 40%. 
Corrected QT interval (Fridericia) ≥450 msec or additional risk factors for QT prolongation. 
Past infection with progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy.

Pregnancy or lactation.
Concurrent participation in another interventional clinical study.
Known symptomatic brain metastases requiring >10 mg prednisolone-equivalent steroids.
History of another primary malignancy (except cured non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, pT1‑2 prostate cancer Gleason <6, or disease‐free ≥1 year).
Major surgery within 4 weeks preceding randomisation.

general_exclusion_criteria:

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Prior exposure to any PSMA‑targeted radionuclide therapy (e.g., ^177Lu‑PSMA‑617).
• Radiotherapy administered within four weeks before Cycle 1 Day 1.
• Any anti‑neoplastic or experimental therapy received within fourteen days before Cycle 1 Day 1.
• Current enrollment in another investigational therapeutic trial.
• Untreated brain metastases (treated brain metastases allowable under stipulated conditions).
• Residual toxicities greater than Grade 1 from preceding anticancer therapy (except alopecia; sensory neuropathy up to Grade 2 accepted).
• Known hypersensitivity to substances chemically related to ^177Lu‑PSMA‑617.
• Active spinal cord compression or imminent threat thereof.
• Ongoing concomitant malignant disease expected to confound safety/efficacy assessments (excluding adequately controlled non‑melanoma skin cancer, superficial bladder cancer, or other cured malignancies meeting predefined intervals).
• Serious or uncontrolled comorbidities such as interstitial lung disease, resting severe dyspnea/on supplemental oxygen, severe renal insufficiency (estimated CrCl < 30 mL/min), inflammatory bowel disorders leading to baseline Grade 2 or worse diarrhea, or extensive gastrointestinal surgery affecting absorption.
• Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

Prior treatment required: at least one endocrine therapy administered in the metastatic setting; up to two chemotherapy regimens in the metastatic setting permitted; up to two prior cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor regimens (excluding abemaciclib) permitted. Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to abemaciclib in the metastatic setting; prior exposure to elacestrant or any investigational selective estrogen receptor degrader/modulator/ceran/proteolysis targeting chimera in the metastatic setting. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor–positive tumour (≥1 % cells stained by IHC) and human epidermal growth factor receptor‑2–negative tumour (IHC 0‑1⁺ or ISH negative) verified during screening. general_exclusion_criteria:
Immediate central nervous system‑specific treatment judged urgently required by the treating physician.
Imminent organ failure or visceral crisis.
Presence of leptomeningeal metastases (CSF cytology positive or definitive radiographic/eclinical evidence).
Breast cancer treatment‑naïve status in the advanced/metastatic setting.
Concurrent malignant neoplasm or malignancy occurring within three years of enrolment (with exceptions for adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer, superficial bladder cancer, cervical carcinoma in situ, or second primary breast cancer).
Participation in another interventional breast cancer clinical trial (unless observational follow‑up only after applicable washout periods).
Unresolved grade > 1 toxicities from prior anticancer therapy (aside from alopecia or peripheral sensory neuropathy graded ≤2).
Severe uncontrolled infections; untreated chronic HBV or HCV infection (undetectable viral load required); HIV infection with detectable viral load prohibited.
Major surgical procedure performed within four weeks preceding initiation of study therapy.
Incapacity to ingest oral medication owing to malabsorption syndromes or other unmanaged gastrointestinal conditions.
Pregnancy, lactation, or inadequate contraception compliance (female participants of child‑bearing potential not employing approved non‑hormonal methods; male participants with fertile partners not utilizing barrier protection or agreeing to refrain from sperm donation as stipulated).
Known hypersensitivity to elacestrant, abemaciclib, or any formulation excipient.
Use of potent or moderate CYP3A4 enzyme inducer or inhibitor agents within a clinically meaningful interval relative to study dosing (timing constraints omitted here as washout periods were disregarded).

Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to abemaciclib in the metastatic setting; prior exposure to elacestrant or any investigational selective estrogen receptor degrader/modulator/ceran/proteolysis targeting chimera in the metastatic setting. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor–positive tumour (≥1 % cells stained by IHC) and human epidermal growth factor receptor‑2–negative tumour (IHC 0‑1⁺ or ISH negative) verified during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Immediate central nervous system‑specific treatment judged urgently required by the treating physician.
Imminent organ failure or visceral crisis.
Presence of leptomeningeal metastases (CSF cytology positive or definitive radiographic/eclinical evidence).
Breast cancer treatment‑naïve status in the advanced/metastatic setting.
Concurrent malignant neoplasm or malignancy occurring within three years of enrolment (with exceptions for adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer, superficial bladder cancer, cervical carcinoma in situ, or second primary breast cancer).
Participation in another interventional breast cancer clinical trial (unless observational follow‑up only after applicable washout periods).
Unresolved grade > 1 toxicities from prior anticancer therapy (aside from alopecia or peripheral sensory neuropathy graded ≤2).
Severe uncontrolled infections; untreated chronic HBV or HCV infection (undetectable viral load required); HIV infection with detectable viral load prohibited.
Major surgical procedure performed within four weeks preceding initiation of study therapy.
Incapacity to ingest oral medication owing to malabsorption syndromes or other unmanaged gastrointestinal conditions.
Pregnancy, lactation, or inadequate contraception compliance (female participants of child‑bearing potential not employing approved non‑hormonal methods; male participants with fertile partners not utilizing barrier protection or agreeing to refrain from sperm donation as stipulated).
Known hypersensitivity to elacestrant, abemaciclib, or any formulation excipient.
Use of potent or moderate CYP3A4 enzyme inducer or inhibitor agents within a clinically meaningful interval relative to study dosing (timing constraints omitted here as washout periods were disregarded).

Prior treatment required: prior standard therapy including radiation therapy and myelosuppressive chemotherapy having been administered and documented as failing; recovery from acute toxic effects of prior therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: SHH molecular subgroup confirmed by CLIA‑certified DNA methylation‑based testing on tumor tissue collected at diagnosis or relapse (assessment performed during screening); adequate pretreatment tumor tissue must be available. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of breastfeeding/nursing motherhood; History of any other malignancy except a secondary brain tumour in remission ≥5 years; Active severe gastrointestinal disorders (difficulty swallowing, malabsorption, uncontrolled diarrhoea, gastritis, inflammatory bowel disease, recent extensive gastrectomy or small‑bowel resections); Clinically significant unrelated systemic illnesses compromising tolerance to therapy; Baseline corrected QTc interval >480 ms; Concurrent use of other anti‑cancer or investigational therapeutic agents; Current anticoagulation with warfarin or lipid‑lowering therapy with statins.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Inflammatory breast cancer. Histology allowed: All invasive breast cancer histologic subtypes. Cancer burden allowed: Clinical stage III inflammatory breast cancer. Prior treatment required: None. Prior treatment excluded: Prior systemic anti‑cancer therapy (chemotherapy, immunotherapy, targeted therapy) for the current diagnosis; prior radiotherapy for the current diagnosis; prior definitive surgery for the current diagnosis (diagnostic biopsies exempt). Biomarkers required: HER2‑positive or HER2‑low (IHC 2+/ISH‑, IHC 1+/ISH‑, or IHC 1+/ISH untested) assessed locally at screening. Biomarkers excluded: HER2‑negative tumors.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of hypersensitivity to durvalumab, trastuzumab deruxtecan, or their constituents; major surgery within 4 weeks preceding treatment; uncontrolled intercurrent illness such as active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmias, severe chronic gastrointestinal disease causing diarrhea, or psychiatric illness limiting compliance; requirement for chronic systemic glucocorticoids >10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent or other immunosuppressants within the past 2 years; active or prior autoimmune or inflammatory disorders (exceptions noted in protocol); previous interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, current ILD/pneumonitis, or inability to rule out ILD/pneumonitis; significant pulmonary comorbidities (pulmonary embolism within 3 months, severe asthma, moderate-to-severe COPD, restrictive lung disease, pleural effusion, rheumatologic lung disease, prior pneumonectomy); corrected QT interval >470 ms or other risk factors for QT prolongation (congenital long QT, electrolyte disturbances, concurrent QT-prolonging medicines); cardiovascular events or procedures within 6 months (coronary artery bypass graft, angioplasty or stent placement, myocardial infarction, angina, NYHA class II or higher heart failure, stroke); left ventricular ejection fraction <50%; other active malignancy apart from adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, or malignancy in remission ≥5 years; venous thromboembolism within the past 3 months; primary immunodeficiency; active infections including untreated tuberculosis, active hepatitis B (HBsAg positive) or hepatitis C (detectable HCV RNA); known HIV infection; receipt of a live vaccine within 30 days before study treatment; use of strong CYP3A4, CYP2C9, or CYP2D6 inhibitor/inducer immediately before dosing; hydroxychloroquine use within 14 days before day 1; leptomeningeal carcinomatosis; pregnancy, breastfeeding, or refusal to use approved contraception throughout study and designated post‑treatment intervals.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 to 59 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia (exclude acute promyelocytic leukemia). Histology allowed: AML classified as high‑risk (adverse) according to European Leukemia Net 2017 criteria, including therapy‑related AML, AML secondary to an antecedent hematologic disorder, and AML with myelodysplasia‑related changes. Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed, untreated disease (baseline blast ≥20%). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior AML‑directed therapy (intensive chemotherapy, targeted agents, or other investigational drugs) apart from hydroxyurea, all‑trans retinoic acid administered solely pending confirmation of APML negativity, a single prophylactic intrathecal chemotherapy dose, prior anthracycline limited to cumulative ≤200 mg/m² dauno­rubicin (or equivalent), and prior hypomethylating-agent exposure when not given for AML diagnosis. Biomarkers required: high‑risk (adverse) AML per ELN 2017 classification. Biomarkers excluded: FLT3 internal tandem duplication or tyrosine kinase domain mutation AND t(9;22) translocation.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of Wilson’s disease or other inherited copper metabolism disorder.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding; unwillingness or inability to use dual contraception methods throughout study participation.
Concurrent receipt of or planned use of other investigational agents before completion of protocol therapy.
Inadequate organ function: creatinine clearance < 30 ml/min, AST or ALT > 3×upper limit of normal, total bilirubin > 2×ULN (unless Gilberts’ syndrome, then ≤5×ULN).
Cardiac left ventricular ejection fraction < 50%.
Uncontrolled infectious conditions not addressed elsewhere in the protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: Chronic lymphocytic leukemia/small lymphocytic lymphoma. Histology allowed: CLL/SLL low‑risk tumor lysis (absolute lymphocyte count < 25×10⁹/L and all nodal lesions < 5 cm). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory low‑risk disease having received ≥ 2 prior systemic therapies. Prior treatment required: At least two prior anticancer regimens (includes BTK inhibitors, PI3K inhibitors, etc.). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of recent autologous or allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplant within 60 days, current immunosuppression, or active graft‑versus‑host disease.
Concurrent active second malignant solid tumors (with exceptions noted for hormone‑responsive breast/prostate cancer, basal cell carcinoma, in‑situ cervical carcinoma, fully resected cancers, or cancers with anticipated survival ≥ 2 years).
Use of any antineoplastic therapy (chemo, radiation, hormonals, biologics, targeted agents) within 14 days or five half‑lives before study drug initiation, or incomplete recovery from prior toxicities greater than Grade 2 (neuropathy exempt).
Recent CYP3A4 strong inducer or inhibitor usage outside designated washout periods, consumption of grapefruit/starfruit derivatives, or live vaccine administration shortly before enrollment.
Significant comorbid organ dysfunction (renal, hepatic, cardiac [NYHA class II or higher], pulmonary, neurological, endocrine, metabolic, infectious) judged unsafe by investigators.
Baseline corrected QT interval ≥ 480 ms, congenital Long QT/Torsade de pointes predisposition, uncontrolled serious arrhythmias, recent myocardial infarction/stroke/coronary artery disease, or severe congestive heart failure.
Uncontrolled infections, active untreated HIV, active hepatitis B or C infection.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Incapacity to ingest oral medication owing to swallowing disorders, GI malabsorption, gastric surgeries, or related conditions.
Known active CNS infiltration by malignancy.
Hypersensitivity to any component of LP‑118 formulation.
Concomitant use of QT‑prolonging/torsadogenic medicines not cleared by sponsor.
Major surgery within 14 days prior to first dose.

Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed/refractory transformed disease, lack of effective options. Prior treatment required: Minimum one prior RTX‑directed regimen. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of recent autologous or allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplant within 60 days, current immunosuppression, or active graft‑versus‑host disease.
Concurrent active second malignant solid tumors (with exceptions noted for hormone‑responsive breast/prostate cancer, basal cell carcinoma, in‑situ cervical carcinoma, fully resected cancers, or cancers with anticipated survival ≥ 2 years).
Use of any antineoplastic therapy (chemo, radiation, hormonals, biologics, targeted agents) within 14 days or five half‑lives before study drug initiation, or incomplete recovery from prior toxicities greater than Grade 2 (neuropathy exempt).
Recent CYP3A4 strong inducer or inhibitor usage outside designated washout periods, consumption of grapefruit/starfruit derivatives, or live vaccine administration shortly before enrollment.
Significant comorbid organ dysfunction (renal, hepatic, cardiac [NYHA class II or higher], pulmonary, neurological, endocrine, metabolic, infectious) judged unsafe by investigators.
Baseline corrected QT interval ≥ 480 ms, congenital Long QT/Torsade de pointes predisposition, uncontrolled serious arrhythmias, recent myocardial infarction/stroke/coronary artery disease, or severe congestive heart failure.
Uncontrolled infections, active untreated HIV, active hepatitis B or C infection.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Incapacity to ingest oral medication owing to swallowing disorders, GI malabsorption, gastric surgeries, or related conditions.
Known active CNS infiltration by malignancy.
Hypersensitivity to any component of LP‑118 formulation.
Concomitant use of QT‑prolonging/torsadogenic medicines not cleared by sponsor.
Major surgery within 14 days prior to first dose.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: Head and neck squamous cell carcinoma located in the oropharynx, oral cavity, hypopharynx, or larynx. Histology allowed: Squamous cell carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Incurable metastatic or recurrent disease (evidence of distant metastasis or regional recurrence not amenable to curative therapy). Prior treatment required: Progressed following anti‑Programmed Death‑1 immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy **and** progressing following platinum‑based chemotherapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Documented p16 status (determined by immunohistochemistry) for tumors of the oropharynx. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Central nervous system metastases that are untreated, symptomatic, or require recent radiation, surgery, or ongoing corticosteroids.
Leptomeningeal disease.
Hypersensitivity reactions to any excipient of the investigational product.
Unstable angina; congestive heart failure classified as New York Heart Association class II‑IV; serious cardiac arrhythmia requiring treatment; myocardial infarction occurring within six months preceding enrollment.
Any prior malignant disease except adequately treated cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, non‑melanoma skin cancer, or other cured cancers judged to pose minimal recurrence risk.
Resting dyspnea of any cause or dependence on continuous supplemental oxygen.
Active severe comorbid conditions—including uncontrolled infections, clinically significant pulmonary, metabolic, or psychiatric disorders.
Presence of known transmissible infectious diseases according to protocol specifications.
Pregnancy or current lactation.
Primary tumor situated in the nasopharynx (regardless of histology).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Endometrial carcinoma. Histology allowed: All endometrial carcinoma histologies except squamous carcinoma and sarcoma. Cancer burden allowed: Recurrent or persistent disease with measurable lesion according to RECIST 1.1 (any stage). Prior treatment required: Minimum ≥1 and maximum ≤2 prior systemic therapy regimens (chemotherapy ± radiotherapy); prior hormonal therapy does not count toward the regimen limit. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy (anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑CTLA‑4) or T‑cell costimulatory agent. Biomarkers required: Presence of tumor tissue sufficient for comprehensive genomic profiling by FoundationOne CDx (assessment performed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Active or historical autoimmune disease or immune deficiency; history of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organising pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, idiopathic pneumonitis, or current pneumonitis on imaging; active tuberculosis; serious infections within four weeks; recent therapeutic antibiotics (except prophylaxis); clinically significant cardiovascular disease; administration of live attenuated vaccine within four weeks or anticipated need during study; prior allogeneic stem cell or solid organ transplantation; recent systemic immunostimulatory agents (interferons, IL‑2) within four weeks or five half‑lives; recent systemic immunosuppressive drugs within two weeks (with permitted low‑dose glucocorticoids for COPD/asthma or adrenal insufficiency); unresolved central nervous system disease, uncontrolled seizure disorder, stroke, transient ischemic attack or subarachnoid haemorrhage within six months.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: mucosal melanoma. Histology allowed: Malignant melanoma arising from mucous membranes. Cancer burden allowed: Resected disease with R0 (negative margin) or R1 (microscopically positive margin) status, no distant metastatic disease on brain/chest/abdomen/pelvis imaging, and must satisfy ONE OF THE FOLLOWING: regional lymph‐node involvement; in‑transit metastases or satellite disease; or a single primary lesion located in head/neck sinusoidal region (any primary, pt4a or higher), nasal or oral cavity, anorectal area, vagina/cervix, urethra/bladder, penis, vulva (cutaneous AJCC stage IIB or higher), esophagus or gallbladder, or locoregionally recurrent disease meeting any of these criteria. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Prior systemic checkpoint inhibitor therapy directed against mucosal melanoma (including adjuvant settings). Biomarkers required: Mandatory central programmed death ligand‑1 (PD‑L1) tumour tissue testing (sample submission and report obtained). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis  
Uncontrolled heart failure or recent acute coronary event  
Severe renal insufficiency (creatinine ≥ 1.5× ULN or CrCl < 30 ml/min)  
Significant hepatic impairment (child‑Pugh B/C)  
Active or uncontrolled central nervous system metastases (unless appropriately treated and stable)  
HIV infection or active hepatitis B/C  
Poor performance status (ECOG > 2)

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (sites: oral cavity, oropharynx, larynx, hypopharynx). Histology allowed: squamous cell carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: locoregionally recurrent disease or second primary occurring in a previously irradiated field, deemed suitable for curative intent surgery; absence of distant metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: prior curative‑intent surgery and/or radiation therapy (± platinum‑containing chemotherapy or cetuximab) completed ≥6 months before documentation of recurrence; where applicable, prior radiation delivered >45 Gy to >50 % of the planned surgical tumour volume. Prior treatment excluded: any prior administration of anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑PD‑L2 agents or other immune‑modulating antibodies targeting checkpoints/co‑stimulatory receptors (e.g., CTLA‑4, OX40, CD137). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of non‑infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease. Active untreated autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression. Current systemic glucocorticoid use greater than 10 mg/day prednisone equivalents. Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease or NYHA Class IIIb-IV heart failure. Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin > upper limits of normal, transaminases > 5× ULN). Severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 ml/min). Untreated central nervous system involvement except stable, adequately managed lesions. Positive serology for HIV infection. Active hepatitis B (surface antigen positive) or hepatitis C infection. Concurrent malignant neoplasm that requires active therapy (except appropriately treated basal/squamous skin carcinomas or carcinoma in situ). Pregnancy or lactation. Inability to comply with study procedures or give informed consent.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: solid tumor. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced unresectable or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: receipt of prior antitumour therapy (standard therapy with documented progression or intolerance, or standard therapy deemed inappropriate, or prior investigational agent considered standard of care). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: KRAS G12C mutation detected in tumor tissue (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Presence of leptomeningeal disease or carcinomatous meningitis  
Clinically significant toxicity resulting from prior cancer therapies  
Known or suspected hypersensitivity to FMC-376 or any components of the study drug  
Condition that would interfere with study drug absorption  
Any illness or medical history that would impact safety or compliance with study requirements or affect ability to interpret study data

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Waldenström macroglobulinemia. Histology allowed: Lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma consistent with Waldenström macroglobulinemia. Cancer burden allowed: Meets International Workshop on Waldenström Macroglobulinemia (IWWM2) treatment criteria (evidence of constitutional symptoms, recurrent fevers, night sweats, fatigue, weight loss, progressive or symptomatic lymphadenopathy or splenomegaly, hemoglobin ≤10 g/dL, platelet count ≤100 k/µL, hyperviscosity syndrome, symptomatic peripheral neuropathy, systemic amyloidosis, renal insufficiency, symptomatic cryoglobulinemia/cold agglutinin disease, or other manifestations warranting therapy). Participation in another investigational study or receipt of investigational agent within 5 half‑lives/4 weeks. Allergies to paclitaxel or DT2216 components (with exceptions for managed re‑challenge). Poor performance status (ECOG >2). Untreated/uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses making protocol adherence unsafe.

Life expectancy ≤ six months.
Laboratory parameters outside protocol-specified limits.
Active invasive malignant disease diagnosed within the previous two years.
Clinically significant or uncontrolled cardiac disease.
Active infection with hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of transformation of CLL to aggressive non‑Hodgkin lymphoma (Richter’s transformation). Allergic reaction to rog​inolisis, venetoclax, or related formulations, or to anti‑CD20 antibodies within 6 months. Active systemic autoimmune diseases. Need for chronic high‑dose immunosuppressants or systemic steroids > 20 mg prednisolone equivalents daily. Pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraceptive measures. Other current malignant neoplasms expected to interfere with study assessments (exceptions apply for adequately treated tumors). Inability to ingest oral medication. Clinically significant cardiovascular disease, including QTcF > 470 msec. Recent (<2 yr) tuberculosis treatment. Current active bacterial, fungal, viral (including hepatitis) or opportunistic infection requiring IV therapy. Documented HIV infection where antiretroviral therapy interferes with venetoclax metabolism. Serious or uncontrolled concomitant illness compromising safe participation. Alcohol or substance dependence.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.
Unresolved toxicities from prior therapy greater than CTCAE grade 1.
Significant cardiovascular disease including unstable angina, acute coronary syndromes, history of myocardial infarction, or documented reduced left ventricular ejection fraction.
Active untreated systemic bacterial, viral, fungal, or parasitic infection.
Known active hepatitis B virus infection or hepatitis C virus infection.
Other concurrent active malignant neoplasm unless in remission with anticipated survival longer than two years.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: Male and female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: 1 to 3 prior chemotherapeutic regimens for metastatic breast cancer, including at least one regimen containing an antibody‑drug conjugate. Prior treatment excluded: Prior progression on platinum‐based chemotherapy in the metastatic setting; prior treatment with a WEE1 kinase inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor negative and Progesterone receptor negative (≤10 % staining by IHC) and Human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative (IHC 0‑1⁺ or ISH ratio < 2.0). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Major surgical procedure less than 28 days before treatment start.
Untreated, symptomatic, or actively progressing central nervous system metastases; recent cranial neurosurgery or brain biopsy within 1 month.
Residual grade ≥ 2 peripheral neuropathy or other grade ≥ 2 toxicity (except alopecia or anorexia) from prior therapy.
Allergic reaction or severe hypersensitivity (grade ≥ 3) attributable to azenosertax, carboplatin, or pembrolizumab or related compounds.
History of non‑infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease requiring steroids, or current pneumonitis/Lung disease.
Presence of a percutaneous endoscopic gastro­stomy tube or dependence on total parental nutrition.
Use of investigational agents or devices within 4 weeks preceding study treatment.
Receipt of live attenuated vaccination within 30 days before first dose (killed vaccines permitted).
Additional malignancies that are progressing or have required active treatment within the past 3 years (exceptions: cured cutaneous carcinomas, low‑risk localized prostate cancer).
Concomitant use of strong/moderate CYP3A4/5 inhibitors, inducers, or P‑glycoprotein modulators, or other medicines known to cause marked QT prolongation.
Uncontrolled comorbid conditions—including active infection, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, serious cardiac arrhythmias, New York Heart Association class III‑IV heart failure, recent myocardial infarction (within 6 months), uncontrolled diabetes, recent peptic ulcer disease, chronic hepatic or renal impairment, severe malnutrition, or psychiatric disorder limiting compliance.
Refractory nausea/vomiting, chronic gastrointestinal disease, or extensive bowel resections compromising oral drug absorption.
Baseline corrected QT interval ≥470 msec or documented congenital long QT syndrome, torsades de pointes susceptibility, or use of medications associated with torsades risk.
Active tuberculosis or other infections necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Systemic autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy within the past two years (aside from physiological replacements) or immunodeficiency; systemic glucocorticoids >10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent or other immunosuppression within 7 days of dosing.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant or sire a child during study period up to 120 days after last dose; inability/unwillingness to employ effective contraception.
Previous allogeneic solid organ or stem cell transplantation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 0 to 21 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Low‑grade glioma. general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease. Uncontrolled congestive heart failure or severe left ventricular systolic dysfunction. Clinically meaningful hepatic impairment (bilirubin > 1.5× upper limit normal or transaminases > 3× ULN). Significant renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min). Active untreated or progressing central nervous system metastasis requiring steroids. Current human immunodeficiency virus infection or chronic hepatitis B/C infection. Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals. Concurrent participation in another therapeutic oncology trial or receipt of prohibited concomitant anticancer drugs. Known or suspected neuroﬁbromatosis type 1. Symptomatic disease lacking radiographic evidence of recurrence or progression.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non‑small cell lung carcinoma (any subtype). Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease with measurable lesions per RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: systemic chemotherapy for metastatic disease. Biomarkers required: activating epidermal growth factor receptor mutation in tumor tissue or circulating free DNA **and** detectable plasma EGFR mutation at Cycle 2 Day 1 (assessment performed during screening/randomization). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Pregnancy or breastfeeding  
Recent radiotherapy within 1 week prior to study treatment onset  
Major surgery within 2 weeks prior to study treatment onset  
Clinically significant interstitial lung disease or history of pulmonary fibrosis/pneumonitis  
Use of strong cytochrome P450 3A4 enzyme inducers that cannot be stopped  
Unresolved adverse events greater than Grade 1 (except alopecia and Grade 2 peripheral sensory neuropathy attributable to prior platinum therapy)  
Severe or uncontrolled systemic illnesses (e.g., uncontrolled hypertension, active hemorrhage)  
Active infections including untreated hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection  
Significant hepatic impairment beyond stipulated laboratory limits or contraindicated antiviral prophylaxis situations  
Inability to tolerate oral administration (refractory nausea/vomiting, extensive bowel resections)  
Baseline QTcF > 470 msec, clinically important ECG abnormalities (complete left bundle branch block, advanced atrioventricular blocks) or coexisting conditions increasing torsade risk (heart failure, electrolytes disturbances, congenital Long QT syndrome, pertinent familial history, interacting QT‑prolonging meds)  
History of drug‑induced interstitial lung disease, radiation‑related pneumonitis requiring corticosteroids, or current active interstitial lung disease  
Known hypersensitivity to osimertinib, its excipients, or structurally related compounds  
Investigation with experimental agent within five half‑lives or three months preceding enrollment  
Failure to demonstrate ability to comply with study protocols and follow‑up requirements.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: small cell carcinoma. Prior treatment excluded: any prior brain radiotherapy or whole‑brain radiotherapy (stereotactic radiosurgery for benign indications acceptable case‑by‑case). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of unstable angina or congestive heart failure requiring hospitalization within 6 months; transpmyocardial infarction within 6 months; acute bacterial or fungal infection necessitating intravenous antibiotics at baseline; chronic obstructive pulmonary disease exacerbation or other acute respiratory illnesses prohibiting study therapy; severe hepatic disease classified as Child–Pugh B or C; human immunodeficiency virus positivity with CD4 count < 200 cells/mm³; contraindications to magnetic resonance imaging (implanted metallic device, ferromagnetic objects); contraindications to gadolinium contrast (allergy, impaired renal function); other active metastatic malignancies currently requiring treatment; pregnancy or breastfeeding; lack of adequate contraception for participants capable of conception; ECOG/Zubrod performance status greater than 2.

general_exclusion_criteria: Participant has a history of malignancy other than soft tissue sarcoma or glioblastoma within 3 years before enrollment (exemptions: squamous or basal cell carcinoma of the skin, carcinoma in situ of the cervix, benign prostatic hypertrophy/neoplasm).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 1 to 39 years. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: Craniopharyngioma. Histology allowed: Any craniopharyngioma histology. Cancer burden allowed: Newly diagnosed disease (tumors identified by imaging, with or without measurable disease). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior tumor‑directed therapy (including surgical resection, cyst drainage, biopsies performed outside of the scheduled diagnostic procedure, radiation therapy, or systemic anticancer therapy). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
- Rapidly progressive neurological symptoms necessitating emergent surgery or radiation.
- Uncontrolled neuroendocrine dysfunction (diabetes insipidus, hypothyroidism, panhypopituitarism) despite stabilized hormone replacement for at least 2 weeks.
- Clinically significant active cardiovascular disease, recent myocardial infarction, venous thromboembolism ≤6 months, cardiomyopathy, or QTcF > 440 ms.
- Severe hypersensitivity reaction to drugs chemically related to Tovarfenib.
- Persistent grade ≥ 2 nausea/vomiting, severe malabsorption, extensive gastrointestinal resections impairing oral drug uptake.
- Ongoing serious infectious processes or unresolved opportunistic infections.
- Concurrent receipt of other investigational medicinal products.
- Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or lack of effective contraception in persons of reproductive potential.
- Concomitant use of potent CYP2C8 inhibitors or inducers not permitted by protocol.
- Inadequate compliance anticipated for follow‑up assessments or study procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥12 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor (locally advanced or metastatic). Histology allowed: Any solid tumor histology. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: One or more prior lines of anticancer therapy with documented progressive disease. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: TP53 Y220C mutation (must be confirmed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: KRAS mutation (single‑nucleotide variant).
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease; recent radiotherapy (<28 days) or chemotherapy (>21 days) (treated as washouts ignored); untreated central nervous system involvement or symptomatic brain metastases requiring steroids; stroke or transient ischemic attack within 6 months; significant cardiac disease (unstable angina, myocardial infarction within 6 months, uncontrolled hypertension, congestive heart failure, prolonged QT, arrhythmias); strong cytochrome P450 3A4 inducer usage; clinically significant gastrointestinal disorders affecting drug absorption; prior organ transplantation; concurrent active malignancies other than adequately treated carcinoma in situ of cervix or non‑melanoma skin cancer; active uncontrolled viral hepatitis B/C or HIV infection; ongoing serious infection requiring systemic therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥12 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor (locally advanced or metastatic). Histology allowed: Any solid tumor histology. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: One or more prior lines of anticancer therapy with documented progressive disease; anti‑PD‑1/PD‑L1 naïve or previously progressed on anti‑PD‑1/PD‑L1 therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Prior anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, or anti‑PD‑L2 agents that were stopped because of a grade ≥3 immune‑related adverse event; prior administration of rezatapopt; receipt of a live or live‑attenuated vaccine within 30 days before first dose; chronic systemic glucocorticoid therapy within 7 days before first dose. Biomarkers required: TP50 Y220C mutation (must be confirmed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: KRAS mutation (single‑nucleotide variant).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma of the prostate without small cell differentiation. Cancer burden allowed: high‑risk localized disease defined by any of the following — PSA > 20 ng/mL (regardless of Gleason score), or Gleason score > 8, or predominant Gleason pattern 4 comprising ≥50% of total length across ≥6 systematic cores (or equivalent distribution in ≥3 systematic/MRI‑targeted cores), or ≥25% Gleason pattern 5 across ≥3 systematic/MRI‑targeted cores; additionally, oligometastatic disease with fewer than three metastases detected exclusively by PSMA PET and judged operable with radical prostatectomy. Excludes stage T4 tumors and visceral/metastatic disease classified as M1 by conventional imaging. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior definitive androgen deprivation therapy, chemotherapy, prostate surgery, or radiation therapy for prostate cancer; more than thirty continuous days of luteinizing hormone‑releasing hormone agonist administered before registration; concurrent or prior (≤6 months) use of exogenous androgens, estrogens, or progesterone analogues. Biomarkers required: availability of prostate tumor tissue sufficient for next‑generation DNA/RNA sequencing/genomic profiling (testing performed during screening) and signed informed consent for genetic testing. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of active infection or chronic liver disease requiring systemic therapy.
Active or known Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection with detectable viral load.
Uncontrolled or recently significant cardiac disease—including recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, symptomatic pericarditis, recent coronary interventions, New York Heart Association class III‑IV heart failure, or major arrhythmia within past year.
Uncontrolled hypertension.
Inability to swallow oral medications or gastrointestinal disorders markedly affecting drug absorption (e.g., extensive resections, malabsorptive syndromes).
Known hypersensitivity to any investigational product components.
Documented non‑compliance with prescribed regimens.
Severe concurrent illness, infection, or comorbidity rendering participation unsafe or interfering with planned radical prostatectomy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Progressive disease after endocrine therapy for hormone receptor‑positive disease; receipt of 1 to 2 prior lines of chemotherapy in the recurrent or metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: Any anti‑HER2 therapy in the metastatic setting; any antibody‑drug conjugate containing an exatecan-derived topoisomerase I inhibitor; any enhancer of zest homologue (EZH) inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Hormone receptor positive (estrogen receptor positive and/or progesterone receptor positive) AND HER2 low (Immunohistochemistry score 1+ OR Immunohistochemistry 2+ with ISH negative). Biomarkers excluded: HER2 positive (Immunohistochemistry 3+ OR Immunohistochemistry 2+ with ISH amplification).
general_exclusion_criteria:
Has uncontrolled or significant cardiovascular disease.
Spinal cord compression or clinically active central nervous system metastases (untreated, symptomatic, or requiring steroid/conventant therapy).
Leptomeningeal carcinomatosis or metastasis.
Clinically severe pulmonary compromise secondary to intercurrent pulmonary illness.
Concurrent use of moderate or strong cytochrome P450 3A inducers.
Systemic glucocorticoid therapy exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent.
History of severe hypersensitivity reaction to monoclonous antibodies.
Ongoing uncontrolled systemic bacterial, fungal, or viral infection necessitating intravenous antimicrobial therapy.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intent to become pregnant during the study (for female participants).
Psychological, social, family, or geographic circumstances precluding reliable follow‑up.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: NA. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Documented VEXAS syndrome with current or past (within 6 months) involvement of at least one organ system (cutaneously, vasculature, musculoskeletal, ocular/periorbital, genitourinary, or pulmonarily). Prior treatment required: Continuous oral glucocorticoid therapy (prednisone or prednisolone) at a stable dose of 15–45 mg/day (or equivalent) at screening (patients on 10–14 mg/day together with another non‑steroidal anti‑inflammatory agent may qualify if glucocorticoid dose is subsequently titrated to 15–45 mg/day). Prior treatment excluded: Allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (at any time); solid organ transplant (excluding corneal grafts); receipt of ≥9 units of packed red blood cells within 90 days preceding enrollment; exposure to hypomethylating agents within 6 months or greater than four cumulative cycles at any time; use of non‑glucocorticoid anti‑inflammatory therapy within protocol‑defined periods; anti‑platelet medications other than low‑dose aspirin (≤100 mg/day) within 28 days; strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers within five half‑lifes of screening; participation in another interventional study or receipt of experimental therapy within the allowable interval. Biomarkers required: Pathogenic somatic UBA1 mutation located at methionine‑41 or adjacent splice‑site variants (c.118‑1, c.118‑2) confirmed by validated next‑generation sequencing, droplet digital PCR, or Sanger sequencing performed on peripheral blood or bone marrow specimens. Biomarkers excluded: High‑risk or very‑high‑risk myelodysplastic syndromes per revised IPSS‑R; diagnosis of multiple myeloma; serum M‑protein ≥3 g/dL; involved/free‑light‑chain ratio ≥100 or involved free light chain concentration ≥100 mg/dL; moderate or severe hepatic impairment classified as Child‑Pugh class B or C; active viral hepatitis; uncontrolled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection (detectable viral load) or untreated HIV; positive quantitative interferon‑gamma release assay (QuantiFERON) at screening; proven disseminated mycobacterial infection.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant cardiovascular disease, including symptomatic heart failure, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, or coronary artery bypass surgery; electrocardiographic findings indicating QTcF >480 msec, ventricular tachyarrhythmia, or advanced atrioventricular block; arterial or venous thrombotic/embolic events (deep‑vein thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, stroke/TIA) occurring within 60 days prior to enrollment; grade ≥ 2 gastrointestinal or mucosal bleeding within 3 months prior to enrollment; moderate or severe hepatic insufficiency (Child‑Pugh B/C) independent of disease‑specific criteria; active hepatitis B or C infection; uncontrolled HIV infection despite therapy; pregnancy, intent to conceive, or nursing/breastfeeding; inability or unwillingness to employ reliable contraceptive methods throughout the study duration and until 90 days post‑last dose; active opportunistic infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy at screening (exceptions permitted for prophylaxis or chronic non‑active regimens); known hypersensitivity to pacritinib or any excipients (microcrystalline cellulose, polyethylene glycol, magnesium stearate); Karnofsky Performance Score <50%; any other condition judged by investigator to jeopardize participant safety or compromise data integrity.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Prostate cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic castration‐resistant prostate cancer with measurable or non‑measurable distant metastases (RECIST v1.1 and/or PCWG3 criteria). Prior treatment required: Prior androgen receptor signalling inhibitor required; prior taxane chemotherapy required unless medically ineligible or refused; prior cabazitaxel plus carboplatin required. Prior treatment excluded: No prior ezh inhibitor; no investigational non‑kinase agents within 4 weeks; no abiraterone/apalutamide/darolutamide within 2 weeks; no enzalutamide within 4 weeks; no radiation therapy within 2 weeks; no major surgery within 4 weeks. Biomarkers required: Qualifying DNA or RNA alteration identified by CLIA‑ certified NGS (specific alteration not stipulated). Biomarkers excluded: None.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of myocardial infarction within 6 months.
Uncontrolled angina within 6 months.
New York Heart Association Class III–IV congestive heart failure.
Corrected QTcF ≥ 470 ms.
Uncontrolled hypertension (SBP >160 mmHg or DBP >100 mmHg).
Clinically significant acute infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Moderate-to-severe hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh C).
Active hepatitis B infection with detectable viral load; unresolved hepatitis C infection.
Untreated or inadequately controlled HIV infection.
Spinal cord compression imminent or established.
Brain metastases or cranial epidural disease not adequately treated/stabilized.
Bleeding diatheses.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: Squamous cell carcinoma of the skin. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or locally advanced disease. Prior treatment required: Prior anti-programmed death 1 therapy (receipt, intolerance, or ineligibility) in the metastatic or locally advanced setting. Prior treatment excluded: Anti‑epidermal growth factor receptor antibody therapy in the unresectable/metastatic setting. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Prior exposure to anti‑EGFR antibodies within 4 weeks of first study dose (excluded).  
• Prior treatment with any anti‑transforming growth factor beta therapy.  
• Grade ≥ 2 hypersensitivity or intolerance to cetuximab, other anti‑EGFR agents, or murine-derived proteins.  
• Current pregnancy or lactation.  
• Requirement for systemic glucocorticoids > 10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent or other immunosuppressants within 14 days of dosing.  
• History of a hematologic malignancy or a solid tumor other than those stipulated, unless definitively treated with no evidence of disease for ≥2 years (exceptions limited to indolent lesions such as basal cell carcinoma, early-stage prostate cancer, cervical intra‑epithelial neoplasia, or ductal carcinoma in situ of the breast).  
• Human immunodeficiency virus infection with CD4⁺ count < 250 cells/µL, lack of stable antiretroviral therapy for ≥4 weeks, or detectable viral load ≥ 400 copies/ml.  
• Chronic hepatitis B infection with active disease not controlled by antivirals.  
• Untreated or actively replicating hepatitis C infection.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Head and neck squamous cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: Squamous cell carcinoma. Prior treatment required: No prior systemic therapy in the recurrent or metastatic setting (therapies delivered >6 months ago as part of multimodal treatment for locally advanced disease are permissible). Prior treatment excluded: Any prior immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy (exception made for neoadjuvant use >6 months prior) and any prior anti‑epidermal growth factor receptor antibody therapy (radiosensitising uses permissible). Biomarkers required: Programmed death ligand‑1 combined positive score ≥1 by approved assay and documented human papillomavirus testing result for oropharyngeal tumours. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

active bleeding diathesis; prior invasive malignancy other than adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, or localized cancer disease‑free ≥3 years; any other severe acute or chronic medical, psychiatric, or laboratory abnormality judged by investigator to pose unacceptable risk or confound outcome assessments.)

general_exclusion_criteria:

- Uncontrolled or symptomatic brain metastases requiring urgent intervention.
- Persistent toxicities from prior systemic anticancer therapy greater than Grade 1 (excluding alopecia and vitiligo).

- Poor functional status (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status higher than 1).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Visceral crisis or impending visceral crisis.
Urgent need for neurosurgical intervention (resection, shunt).
Immediate indication for local CNS therapy.
Symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage (>2 seizures within 4 weeks prior to enrolment).
Ongoing grade >1 toxicity from prior anticancer therapy (excluding alopecia).
Contraindication to MRI (unless CT usable per cohort specifications).
Concurrent administration of other antitumour agents during the study.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (active infection, severe pulmonary compromise, unstable angina, uncontrolled arrhythmias, recent MI, recent GI ulcers, chronic hepatic/renal disease, severe malnutrition, unmanaged diabetes monitoring inability).
Requirement for systemic corticosteroid >4 mg prednisone equivalent daily or other immunosuppression within 7 days pre-baseline MRI (stable low-dose steroids ≤4 mg allowed).
History of non‑infectious interstitial lung disease / pneumonitis requiring steroids, current ILD/pneumonitis, or unresolved/suspected ILD/pneumonitis.
Uncontrolled seizures, serious CNS disorders, or disabling psychiatric conditions impairing compliance.
Other malignant neoplasms except adequately resected non‑melanoma skin cancer, cured in‑situ disease, or other solid tumours disease‑free ≥3 years.
Major surgery, open biopsy, or significant trauma within 28 days preceding study start or anticipated during study.
Clinically significant corneal disease.
Severe hypersensitivity reaction to datipotamab deruxtecan components or polysorbitol 80.
Severe hypersensitivity to other monoclonal antibodies.
Pregnancy or lactation; lack of a valid negative pregnancy test for women of childbearing potential.
Failure to meet contraception requirements for fertile men or women (required sterilization or reliable barrier/highly effective contraception).

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of solid organ transplantation.
Active central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis.
Clinically significant untreated autoimmune disease requiring systemic steroid therapy equal to or greater than prednisolone 10 mg daily.
Recent uncontrolled cardiovascular conditions (symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction, stroke, or ventricular arrhythmia within six months).
Presence of another concurrent malignancy that is currently progressive or requires active treatment (exceptions: cured cutaneous basal cell carcinoma, cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma, or in‑situ cervical cancer).
Laboratory‐confirmed chronic hepatitis B or C infection (positive HBsAg or anti‑HCV without RNA negativity).
Pregnant or nursing individuals, or persons planning conception during the study period.

Active infections requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.

Performance status greater than ECOG 1.

Clinically significant hepatic dysfunction.
Clinically significant renal insufficiency.
Uncontrolled extracranial disease progression.
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection or active hepatitis B/C infection.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Any malignant neoplasm. Histology allowed: NA. general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or breastfeeding; body weight greater than 120 kg; history of total proctocolectomy; colon perforation or abdominal abscess; partial or complete bowel obstruction within the last three months, symptoms suggestive of obstruction, or imaging showing impending obstruction; active Clostridioides difficile infection or other colonic infection; concurrent hepatitis B or C infection; untreated latent or active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection or lack of appropriate prophylaxis; active or prior infection with non‑tuberculous mycobacteria; inability or refusal to undergo colonoscopy or flexible sigmoidoscopy; inpatient status at the time of planned treatment (subject must be outpatient); allergic reaction to sulfamethoxazole‑trimethoprim; inability to provide informed consent; any condition judged by the investigator to make participation unsafe.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma. Histology allowed: World Health Organization grade IV IDH‑wildtype glioblastoma, gliosarcoma, or IDH‑wildtype diffuse astrocytic tumour with EGFR amplification and/or TERT promoter mutation and/or combined chromosome 7 gain/chromosome 10 loss. Cancer burden allowed: first or second relapse of glioblastoma with radiographic progression according to Modified RANO criteria; no requirement for imminent surgical resection. Prior treatment required: up‑front radiation therapy with or without temozolomide and ≤1 additional anticancer therapy administered for first recurrence (maximum two prior anticancer regimens overall). Prior treatment excluded: prior receipt of any poly(ADP‑ribose) polymerase inhibitor, any anti‑vascular endothelial growth factor directed agent (e.g., bevacizumab, cediranib, aflibercept, vandetanib, cabozantinib, sunitinib), any anti‑programmed death‑1/programmed death‑ligand‑1/lower‑dose programmed death ligand‑2 antibody, any anti‑C​TLA‑4 antibody (including ipilimumab), prior virotherapy or vaccine therapy directed against glioblastoma. Biomarkers required: IDH‑wildtype status demonstrated by immunohistochemistry and/or DNA sequencing; absence of 1p/19q co‑deletion. Biomarkers excluded: IDH‑mutated tumours; tumours harbouring 1p/19q co‑deletion.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of actively progressing secondary malignancy (excluding adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, localized prostate cancer not requiring therapy, or cured in‑situ cervical cancer).
Active uncontrolled infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Known HIV infection, active hepatitis B or C infection.
Unresolved diarrhoea ≥grade 2, active inflammatory bowel disease, or other gastrointestinal disorder compromising drug absorption.
Significant cardiac disease (recent myocardial infarction ≤6 months, NYHA class III‑IV heart failure, unstable angina, symptomatic arrhythmias, prolonged QTc ≥500 ms, myocarditis, pericarditis, or structural valve disease causing functional limitation).
Recent major surgery (<4 weeks) or incomplete recovery from preceding operative complications.
Concurrent high‑dose systemic glucocorticoids (dex >2 mg/day or equivalent) for ≥3 continuous days within 2 weeks before study start, or other systemic immunosuppressive agents within 6 months.
Use of enzyme‑inducing antiseizure medicines, potent CYP3A4 inducers/inhibitors, or prohibited herbals/pharmaceutical supplements within stipulated washout periods.
Therapeutic anticoagulation with vitamin K antagonists (warfarin/coumarins) at enrolment.
Past or present myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukaemia, or features suggesting transformation toward these entities.
Evidence of diffuse leptomeningeal spread or extraneural/metastatic disease.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Live vaccination within 30 days prior to initiation of study therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Renal cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: All histological subtypes of renal cell carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Node‑positive unresectable disease (TxN1Mx) **or** metastatic disease (TxNxM1) with measurable extracranial disease per RECIST v1.1; International Metastatic Database Consortium (IMDC) intermediate risk (**or**) poor risk. Prior treatment required: Candidate for standard‑of‑care immunotherapy consisting of either an immuno‑oncology doublet (e.g., nivolumab plus ipilimumab) **or** an immuno‑oncology plus vascular endothelial growth factor receptor tyrosine kinase inhibitor combination (e.g., axitinib with pembro­luzim ab) beginning at enrolment; measurable disease outside the primary renal tumour; primary renal tumour ≤20 cm maximum anterior‑posterior diameter. Prior treatment excluded: Any systemic therapy for metastatic renal cell carcinoma initiated >90 days before registration; prior radiotherapy to the affected kidney that would overlap future SABR fields; planned definitive curative treatment of **all** metastatic sites; untreated or unstable brain metastases or cranial epidural disease; prior radical/local therapy resulting in complete eradication of all measurable extrarenal disease. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Untreated or unstable brain metastases; prior definitive treatment of all metastatic sites making extra‑renal disease absent; prior overlapping radiotherapy to the kidney; systemic therapy for metastatic RCC started >90 days pre‑registration; active autoimmune disease needing systemic steroids (>10 mg/day prednisolone equivalent); severe allergy to therapeutic antibodies; active tuberculosis; uncontrolled hypertension (SBP ≥190 mmHg or DBP >110 mmHg); major surgery ≤28 days before registration; serious non‑healing wound, ulcer, or bone fracture ≤45 days; arterial thrombotic event (MI, stroke, etc.) ≤180 days; NYHA class III‑IV congestive heart failure; moderate or severe hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh B/C); symptomatic pulmonary embolism or DVT ≤180 days (treated/asymptomatic PE/DVT >30 days acceptable); unstable cardiac arrhythmia ≤180 days; recent (<180 days) abdominal/fecal/perforation/gastrointestinal obstructive episode; active inflammatory bowel disease; malabsorptive syndrome ≤45 days; pregnancy or breastfeeding; inability to practice effective contraception.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥65 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Glioblastoma. Histology allowed: World Health Organization grade IV glioblastoma, IDH wild‑type. History of clinically significant pulmonary toxicity including pneumonitis.
Uncontrolled congestive heart failure or symptomatic cardiac disease.
Severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance markedly reduced).

Active untreated central nervous system infection or meningitis.
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection or chronic viral hepatitis B/C with detectable viremia.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Concurrent participation in another interventional oncology trial.
Any condition judged by investigator to preclude compliance with study assessments or jeopardize participant safety.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• History of Fanconi anaemia, Shwachman‑Diamond syndrome, constitutional trisomy 21 or mosaic trisomy 21, telomere biology disorders, or any inherited germline predisposition increasing toxicity risk.  
• Concurrent malignant disease.  
• Diagnosis of juvenile myelomonocytic leukaemia, Philadelphia‑chromosome‑positive AML, mixed‑phenotype acute leukaemia, acute promyelocytic leukaemia, AML evolving from myelodysplastic syndromes, or therapy‑related myeloid neoplasms.  
• Persistent left ventricular systolic dysfunction prior to enrolment (ejection fraction < 50 % or fractional shortening < 24%).  
• Congenital long QT syndrome or congenital atrioventricular conduction block (relevant for gilteritinib arms).  
• Pregnancy or breastfeeding; lactating women unwilling to abstain from nursing for prescribed periods.  
• Lack of agreement to use effective contraception throughout treatment and designated post‑treatment intervals for sexually active participants of reproductive potential.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of another malignant disorder within the past 2 years (exceptions: adequately treated carcinoma in situ, localized breast cancer cured with surgery/adjuvant hormone therapy and free of disease >3 years, localized Gleason ≤6 prostate adenocarcinoma under surveillance or ADT, or any other solid tumour deemed low recurrence risk by investigator). Ongoing systemic therapy for any other malignancy. Requirement for continuous systemic glucocorticoid therapy ≥10 mg prednisone equivalents per day. History or current involvement of the central nervous system (brain, spine, meninges, CSF) by B‑cell malignancy. Presence of any of the following hematologic/plasma‑cell/neoplastic conditions: active plasma cell myeloma, prolymphocytic leukemia, T‑cell lymphoma, Burkitt lymphoma, AIDS‑associated B‑cell lymphoma, Castleman's disease, post‑transplant lymphoproliferative disorder, hair­y cell leukemia, high‑grade B‑cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 and/or BCL6 rearrangements, high‑grade B‑cell lymphoma—not otherwise specified, B‑cell lymphoma unclassifiable with features intermediate between DLBCL and classic Hodgkin lymphoma, or any other lymphoma entity expressly excluded in the protocol. Poor performance status (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group greater than 2). Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease, significant hepatic impairment, severe renal insufficiency, active infection with HIV, hepatitis B or C, or any serious medical condition precluding compliance with study procedures.
