clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Salivary gland carcinoma. Histology allowed: All histologic subtypes. Cancer burden allowed: Resectable stage II (positive margins), stage III, stage IVA, or stage IVB locoregionally advanced disease per AJCC 8th edition, with no distant metastasis. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Prior radiation and/or chemotherapy for head‐neck cancer; any prior HER2‑directed therapy. Biomarkers required: HER2 positive (tumor HER2 immunohistochemistry 2+ or 3+ **or** HER2 amplification by fluorescence in situ hybridisation [HER2/CEP17 ratio ≥ 2.0 or mean copy number ≥ 4.0] **or** pathogenic HER2/ERBB2 mutation detected on tumour genomic sequencing; biomarker assessment performed on pretreatment tumour material. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Active or uncontrolled infection.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses such as symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, serious cardiac arrhythmia, or severe psychiatric disorder impairing compliance.
Presence of a second active malignancy requiring systemic therapy (excluding adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, in‑situ cervical cancer, or low‑risk prostate cancer on active surveillance).
Any other conditions deemed unsafe by investigator.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Recurrent, advanced (unresectable) or metastatic disease; stable central nervous system metastases permitted. Prior treatment required: At least one and no more than two prior lines of systemic therapy for metastatic/advanced disease (patients who have exhausted all standard‑of‑care options are also eligible). Prior treatment excluded: Any prior experimental agent targeting the KRAS, BRAF, MEK, or ERK pathway. Biomarkers required: KRAS non‑G12C mutation (including G12V, G12D, G13 variants, Q61 variants). Biomarkers excluded: MEK1/2 mutation.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Major surgical procedure performed within four weeks before enrollment or anticipated during the study.
Ongoing anticancer chemotherapy or biologic therapy.
Ongoing radiation therapy.
Active, clinically significant bacterial, fungal, or viral infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Symptomatic spinal cord compression.
Evidence of another active malignant disease requiring systemic therapy within the past two years.
History of retinal vein occlusion or existing risk factors for retinal vein occlusion.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Concurrent participation in another interventional clinical trial.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease, severe hepatic impairment, severe renal insufficiency, or inadequate hematologic reserve (performance status >2).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 1 to less than 22 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: B‑Acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: Precursor B‑cell lineage. Cancer burden allowed: Bone marrow blasts ≥5% (M2 or M3) with or without extramedullary disease. Prior treatment required: Second or greater relapse **OR** primary refractory disease with ≥2 prior induction attempts **OR** first relapse refractory to ≥1 re‑induction attempt; prior hematopoietic stem‑cell transplant allowable (≤1 transplant) and must have occurred ≥90 days before enrollment. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Surface CD22 expression on leukemic blasts demonstrated at relapse by flow cytometry or immunohistochemistry; for Philadelphia chromosome‑positive disease, presence of BCR‑ABL1 fusion (**must**) and having received two different tyrosine kinase inhibitors. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
* History of sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (SOS)
* Isolated central nervous system, testicular, or other extramedullary sites of relapse
* Prior treatment with inotuzuman ozogamicin
* Current participation in another interventional investigational drug study
* Ongoing systemic anticancer therapy (excluding hydroxyurea and prescribed intrathecal chemotherapy)
* Concurrent antirejection or anti‑graft‑versus‑host‑disease medication (cyclosporine, tacrolimus, etc.)
* Uncontrolled infectious diseases (active bacteremia, febrile illness, recent fungemia, untreated viral/protozoal infection)
* Known inherited bone‑marrow failure syndromes (Bloom, ataxia‑telangiectasia, Fanconi anemia, Kostmann, Shwachman‑Diamond, etc.)
* Human immunodeficiency virus infection
* Hepatitis B or C infection
* Pregnancy or lactation; insufficient contraception compliance for participants of reproductive potential
* Significant hepatic impairment (direct bilirubin >1.5× ULN, ALT >5× ULN) or renal impairment (creatinine clearance <70 mL/min/1.73 m²)

Histology allowed: Non‑small cell lung cancer (excluding tumors with small‑cell, large‑cell neuroendocrine, sarcomatoid, or pancoast morphological features). Cancer burden allowed: Pathologic stage II, stage IIIA, or stage IIIB (N2) disease that is deemed resectable, with postoperative confirmation of residual viable tumor (i.e., no pathological complete response) after curative-intent surgery. Prior treatment required: Completion of neoadjuvant platinum‑doublet chemotherapy concurrently with pembrolizumab, followed by surgical resection. Prior treatment excluded: Any systemic anticancer therapy other than the specified neoadjuvant platinum‑based doublet chemotherapy plus pembrolizumab; any prior exposure to anti‑programmed cell death‑1, PD‑L1, PD‑L2, CTLA‑4, OX‑40, CD137, or other immune‑checkpoint modulators beyond the mandated neoadjuvant pembrolizumab; any prior cancer vaccine; any prior investigational anticancer agent not detailed in the protocol; any prior radiotherapy intended as therapeutic (as opposed to diagnostic/staging) irrespective of interval. Biomarkers required: Confirmed absence of activating epidermal growth factor receptor exon 19 deletions or L858R substitution AND confirmed absence of anaplastic lymphoma kinase gene rearrangement. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Receipt of radiotherapy within two weeks prior to initiation of study medication or existing radiation‑related toxicities requiring corticosteroid support.
Administration of a live or live‑attenuated vaccine within thirty days prior to the first dose of study intervention.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥1 year. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Epstein‑Barr virus–associated lymphoproliferative disorder occurring in individuals with primary immunodeficiency. Histology allowed: Lymphomatous or non‑malignant lymphoproliferative lesion (diagnosed by biopsy or positive cerebrospinal fluid cytology). Cancer burden allowed: Can have systemic disease only, systemic plus central nervous system disease, or central nervous system disease only; disease evaluable by Lugano classification. Prior treatment required: One prior line of systemic therapy (required for relapsed/refractory disease) together with radiographic disease progression per Lugano criteria during/after treatment or failure to attain a complete or partial response after standard first‑line therapy. Prior treatment excluded: History of prior allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplantation or solid organ transplant. Biomarkers required: Presence of Epstein‑Barr virus in lesional tissue (e.g., EBER in situ hybridization) or in cerebrospinal fluid. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of actively treating another malignancy such as Burkitt, T‑cell, NK/T‑cell lymphoma, Hodgkin lymphoma, plasmablastic lymphoma, transformed lymphoma, or any other cancer requiring systemic therapy. Current severe uncontrolled infections including adenoviral infection or infections necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy. Known HIV infection. Grade ≥ 2 acute graft‑versus‑host disease or extensive chronic graft‑versus‑host disease. Requirement for vasopressor or mechanical ventilation support at baseline. Pregnancy or lactation. Inadequate compliance with contraception or reproductive restrictions. Daily corticosteroid usage exceeding 0.5 mg/kg prednisolone equivalents (except protocol‑allowed peri‑enrollment dexamethasone for CNS disease). Life expectancy less than 60 days or any condition posing unacceptable risk to study outcome. Poor performance status outside allowable limits (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group ≤ 3 for adults, Lansky ≥ 20 for children). Significant organ dysfunction not attributed to the underlying EBV‑linked disease.

* Significant cardiovascular events within the past 6 months.  
* Current interstitial lung disease requiring therapy.  
* Recent major surgical wounds (within 30 days) or minor surgeries (within 10 days).  
* Active infections with HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C.  
* Baseline hepatic impairment classified as Child‑Pugh B or C.  
* Poor performance status (e.g., ECOG > 2).  
* Concurrent participation in another therapeutic oncology trial.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Female and Male. Cancer type allowed: Non-inflammatory invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Early stage, node-positive, no distant metastasis, high risk of recurrence. Prior treatment required: Minimum 24 months and maximum 60 months of adjuvant endocrine therapy (tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitor) with or without CDK 4/6 inhibitor. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor positive (≥10%) **and** HER2 negative (IHC 0/1 or IHC 2 with ISH negative). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of any prior (ipsilateral and/or contralateral) invasive breast cancer  
Malignancy within three years before randomisation, except adequately treated basal or squamous cell skin cancer, superficial bladder cancer, or cervical intraepithelial neoplasia/carcinoma in situ  
More than six consecutive months’ interruption of prior standard-of-care adjuvant endocrine therapy or discontinuation of adjuvant endocrine therapy greater than six months before randomisation

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >1 year and <25 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: B‑cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: B‑cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia, high‑risk features per protocol. Cancer burden allowed: Bone marrow infiltration ≥25% blasts (or equivalent diagnostic evidence); White blood cell count criteria – Age 1‑9.99 years: WBC ≥ 50,000/µL OR (WBC < 50,000/µL WITH testicular leukemia OR CNS3 leukemia OR steroid pretreatment); Age 10‑24.99 years: any WBC. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Cytotoxic chemotherapy (excluding steroid pretreatment, steroid cytoreduction, intrathecal cytarabine); hydroxyurea >72 hours within seven days prior to protocol start; radiation therapy at diagnosis. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: MYC translocation associated with mature (Burkitt) B‑cell ALL.
general_exclusion_criteria:
- History of Down syndrome.
- Insufficient diagnostic bone marrow specimen for mandatory molecular profiling or lacking peripheral blood sample with >1,000/µL circulating leukemic cells.
- Acute undifferentiated leukemia.
- T‑lymphoblastic lymphoma.
- Morphologically unclassifiable lymphoma.
- Lack of both B‑cell and T‑cell phenotype markers in a case submitted as lymphoblastic lymphoma.
- Known Charcot‑Marie‑Toe disease.
- Pregnancy (female participants).
- Breastfeeding/Lactation (women planning to nurse infant during study and for two months after last dose).
- Failure to agree to/use effective contraception throughout study participation (for sexually active persons of reproductive potential).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: melanoma. Histology allowed: malignant melanoma. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable stage III or stage IV melanoma (measurable disease per RECIST 1.1; no untreated active central nervous system metastases; treated brain metastases allowable if stable). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior ipilimumab in the metastatic setting and prior anti‑PD‑1 or anti‑PD‑L1 therapy in the metastatic setting. Biomarkers required: BRAF mutational status determination (either wild‑type or mutant). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or breastfeeding; lack of adequate contraception for individuals of child‑bearing potential; history of solid tumors other than adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer, in‑situ cervical cancer, or DCIS/lobular carcinoma in situ; active infectious diseases including HIV, active hepatitis B or C (cleared carriers allowed); uncontrolled cardiovascular disease, congestive heart failure, pulmonary hypertension, interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis; hepatic impairment exceeding laboratory limits; renal impairment with CrCl < 40 mL/min; baseline hematologic abnormalities outside stipulated ranges; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression or chronic systemic steroids; historical symptomatic autoimmune disorder (rheumatoid arthritis, systemic scleroderma, SLE, vasculitis, MS, GBS, Myasthenia Gravis); inflammatory bowel disease or diverticulitis; receipt of live vaccinations within prohibited window; participation in another investigational study within disqualifying interval; requirement for medications that could confound safety assessments (e.g., chronic anticoagulant with INR ≥ 1.5); uncontrolled brain metastases; performance status worse than ECOG 0‑1.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 12 months. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: Wilms tumor (nephroblastoma). Histology allowed: any. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of uncontrolled infection.  
Received prior selinexor or any other Exportin‑1 (XPO1) inhibitor.  
Active graft‑vs‑host disease secondary to prior allogeneic bone‑marrow transplantation.  
Prior solid‑organ transplantation.  
Unable to comply with study assessments or follow‑up owing to severe medical, psychiatric, or socioeconomic factors.  
Pregnancy or currently breast‑feeding.  
Refusal or inability to employ two reliable forms of contraception (or abstinence) for the duration of the study.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: any. Cancer type allowed: Waldenström macroglobulinemia (lymphoplasma­cytic lymphoma). Histology allowed: lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma (Waldenström macroglobulinemia). Cancer burden allowed: symptomatic disease necessitating treatment according to International Workshop on Waldenström’s Macroglobulinemia version 2 criteria (e.g., hyperviscosity, peripheral neuropathy, cold agglutinin disease, cryoglobulinemia, amyloid deposition, cytopenias from marrow infiltration, bulky nodal disease, symptomatic splenomegaly, unexplained constitutional symptoms, or organ dysfunction attributable to Waldenström macroglobulinemia). Prior treatment required: receipt of an anti‑CD20 antibody (e.g., rituximab) **and** a Bruton’s Tyrosine Kinase inhibitor (e.g., ibrutinb​in, zanubrutinib) in any sequence, comprising at least one prior line of therapy discontinued because of intolerance or disease progression. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: tumor CD20 positivity by immunophenotype or immunohistochemistry **and** serum IgM >2 times the upper limit of normal at screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Participants whose disease has transformed to aggressive lymphoma; individuals with symptomatic or refractory hyperviscosity syndrome or IgM >4000 mg/dL who cannot receive/plasmaphere; enrollment while taking any other investigational anticancer agent; unrecovered grade >1 toxicities from prior therapy (except alopecia and peripheral neuropathy); untreated active infections requiring inpatient care or iv antibiotics within four weeks; uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (NYHA class III–IV heart failure, left ventricular ejection fraction <45 %, unstable angina, myocardial infarction < six months, serious arrhythmias); significant central nervous system pathology (uncontrolled seizures, recent stroke, severe neuropsychiatric disease, CNS vasculitis, encephalitis); resting supplemental oxygen requirement to keep SpO₂ > 92 %; chronic systemic steroid use ≥20 mg prednisolone equivalent daily or other potent immunosuppressants within 28 days; documented central nervous system involvement or leptomeningeal disease from Waldenström macroglobulinemia; pregnant or lactating persons or anyone refusing mandated contraception; inability to comply with contraception requirements; current alcohol/substance misuse, unmanaged psychiatric illness, or socially unstable circumstances limiting adherence; hypersensitivity to substances chemically or biologically similar to epcoritamab; exposure to live or live‑attenuated vaccination within four weeks.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: Plasma cell malignant neoplasm. Cancer burden allowed: Progressive disease after latest therapy as defined by International Myeloma Working Group criteria. Prior treatment required: At least 3 previous systemic therapy lines. Prior treatment excluded: Receipt of any BCMA‑directed therapy. Biomarkers required: Measurable serum or urinary M‑protein or involved free light‐chain ratio meeting laboratory standards. Biomarkers excluded: None.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Received an autologous hematopoietic stem cell transplant within 12 weeks preceding first dose, or an allogenic transplant within 1 year preceding first dose.
Unresolved grade ≥2 toxicities attributable to prior anticancer therapy.
Presence of non‑secretory multiple myeloma.
Active plasma cell leukemia (>20 % circulating plasma cells or >2.0×10⁹/L circulating plasma cells).
Waldenström macroglobulinemia.
Light‑chain amyloidosis.
Polyneuropathy, organomegaly, endocrine disorder, monoclonal protein, skin changes (POEMS) syndrome.
Major surgical procedure performed ≤4 weeks before first dose or scheduled during study participation.
Acute bacterial, fungal, or viral infection requiring antimicrobial therapy within 14 days before first dose.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus or arterial hypertension within 14 days before first dose.
Peripheral sensory/motor neuropathy grade ≥3, or grade ≥2 accompanied by pain occurring within 2 weeks before first dose.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: clear cell carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable or metastatic disease; evaluable disease per RECIST version 1.1 (dose‐escalation) or measurable disease per RECIST 1.1 (expansion). Prior treatment required: disease refractory to standard therapy, intolerant to standard therapy, or decline of standard therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: PIK3CA mutation identified in blood and/or tumor (assessment conducted during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of type I or II diabetes requiring antiglycaemic medications or fasting plasma glucose ≥ 140 mg/dL or HbA₁c ≥ 7.0%.
Hypersensitivity or serious allergic reaction to any PI3K inhibitor class.
In combination arms only: hypersensitivity to fulvestrant, palbociclib, ribociclib, or PF‑07220060.
Past or current interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring therapeutic intervention (grade 1 resolved pneumonitis allowable except in triple‑combo arms).
Clinically significant cardiac abnormalities: mean resting corrected QT interval > 470 ms (or ≥ 450 ms for ribociclib-containing regimens), personal or family history of congenital Long‑Qt syndrome or Torsades de Pointes.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease.
Active central nervous system metastases or primary CNS malignancy causing progressive neurological deficits.

general_exclusion_criteria:

Active infection requiring systemic antimicrobials at enrolment.
Major surgery or significant trauma within 4 weeks before starting study drug or incomplete recovery from such procedure.
Requirement for chronic warfarin or other vitamin K antagonist anticoagulation.
History of severe inherited bleeding disorders (hemophilia, Von Willebrand disease) or unexplained recurrent bleeding necessitating transfusion.
Stroke or intracranial haemorrhage within 6 months of first dose.
Life‑threatening haemorrhage within 3 months of first dose.
Uncontrolled autoimmune haemolytic anaemia or immune thrombocytopaenia not attributable solely to lymphoma.
Active hepatitis C infection.
Active hepatitis B infection.
Other concurrent malignant disease unless cured with curative intent and disease free ≥3 years (exceptions: non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ of cervix or breast, Gleason 6 prostate cancer observed).
New York Heart Association class III–IV congestive heart failure.
Myocardial infarction within 6 months, unstable angina within 3 months, or clinically significant ventricular arrhythmias/sustained VT/VF/torsade de pointe within 6 months.
Mobitz II second‑degree or complete atrioventricular block without a permanent pacemaker.
Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic > 170 mmHg and diastolic > 105 mmHg on two successive readings).
Baseline corrected QT interval Fridericia > 480 ms.
Inability to swallow capsules or significant GI malabsorption syndromes.
Concomitant use of strong CYP3A inducers.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intention to conceive during the study.
Any severe or uncontrolled comorbidity compromising safe participation or compliance with protocol requirements.

Breastfeeding.
Non‑adherence to prescribed medication regimes.
Unable or unwilling to follow protocol requirements.
Participation in another interventional clinical trial within the prior 30 days.

Participation in another interventional clinical trial within 4 weeks prior to dosing.
Clinically significant laboratory or physical findings deemed by investigators to compromise safety or data interpretation.
Psychiatric, psychosocial, geographic, or logistical circumstances interfering with adherence to study assessments.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Ovary, fallopian tube, or peritoneal cancer. Prior treatment required: Failure of all standard therapies (platinum‑based chemotherapy ± bevacizumab as indicated) with ≥ 1 prior systemic regimen; no prior receipt of bevacizumab. Prior treatment excluded: Bevacizumab; any CD73‑targeting or A₂A receptor‑targeting agent. Biomarkers required: None. Biomarkers excluded: None.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Concurrent malignancy within < 5 years (exceptions: adequately treated CIS cervix, cutaneous SCC, basal cell carcinoma, localized prostate cancer, DCIS of breast, <T1 urothelial carcinoma).
Active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (stable, asymptomatic brain mets permissible under protocol limits).
Recent major cardiovascular events (stroke, TIA, MI, unstable angina) or NYHA class III/IV heart failure within 6 months; QTcF > 480 ms; uncontrolled arrhythmia within 3 months.
Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy within < 2 years (certain endocrine disorders exempt).
History of allogeneic stem-cell or solid-organ transplantation.
Severe IgE‑mediated or grade 3‑4 allergic reactions to monoclonal antibodies or protein therapeutics.
Grade 3‑4 immune‑related adverse event leading to discontinuation of prior therapy (endocrinopathy manageable with hormone replacement exempt).
Systemic glucocorticoids ≥10 mg prednisone daily (or equivalent) within 2 weeks pre‑screening (topical/inhaled low‑dose allowed).
Antibiotic course longer than 1 week within 28 days preceding dosing.
Receipt of any live attenuated vaccine within 4 weeks before first dose.
Positive SARS‑CoV‑2 PCR or antigen screen at enrollment (eligible after isolation/quarantine once negative).
Active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection currently on therapy or completed therapy within 1 year.
Major surgical procedure, ablative/intervention, or systemic therapy within 4 weeks before first dose (diagnostic biopsies exempt).
Participation in another interventional clinical trial within 4 weeks prior to dosing.
Clinically significant laboratory or physical findings deemed by investigators to compromise safety or data interpretation.
Psychiatric, psychosocial, geographic, or logistical circumstances interfering with adherence to study assessments.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥1 year and ≤21 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: all morphologic variants of acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Cancer burden allowed: relapse (second or greater) **or** refractory after ≥2 chemotherapy cycles **or** first relapse after primary chemotherapy‑refractory disease; requires ≥5 % blasts in bone marrow; **no central nervous system disease**. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: previous tagraxofusp exposure. Biomarkers required: surface CD123 expression demonstrated by flow cytometry or immunohistochemistry at screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Uncontrolled cardiac disease including symptomatic heart failure or left ventricular ejection fraction <50%.
Severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance <70 mL/min/1.73 m²) or dialysis dependence.
Significant hepatic dysfunction (bilirubin >1.5× ULN or transaminases >3× ULN despite adequate function elsewhere).
Active uncontrolled central nervous system metastasis or leptomeningeal disease.
Known HIV infection or active hepatitis B or C infection.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Performance status poorer than Karnofsky ≤50 % (ages >16) or Lansky ≤50 % (≤16).
Concurrent serious infection (positive blood/fungal cultures, febrile neutropenia, ongoing intravenous antimicrobial therapy).
Allergy to any study medication components.
Ongoing use of investigational agents or other antitumor therapies not expressly permitted.
Use of anti‑GVHD or organ‑rejectant prophylaxis (cyclosporine, tacrolizumab, etc.) within four weeks of enrolment.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Pathologic stage T1c–T3, node‐negative (pN0, including isolated tumor cells), no distant metastasis. Prior treatment required: Completion of definitive surgery (total mastectomy, skin‑sparing mastectomy, nipple‑sparing mastectomy, or lumpectomy) with microscopically negative margins (except allowable cases noted) and axillary staging by sentinel node biopsy, targeted axillary dissection, or axillary lymph node dissection. Prior treatment excluded: Any systemic chemotherapy, radiation therapy, or biologic therapy directed at the current breast cancer; endocrine therapy for the current cancer lasting longer than six weeks; prior radiation therapy to the affected breast/chest wall; prior systemic anticancer therapy (other than permitted short‑course endocrine therapy ≤6 wk). Biomarkers required: Estrogen‑receptor positive (≥1% by IHC) and/or progesterone‑receptor positive (≥1% by IHC); human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative by ASCO/CAP criteria; Oncotype DX recurrence score 21–25 **or** (Recurrence score 16–20 **and** high clinical risk defined as low grade & tumor >3 cm, or intermediate grade & tumor >2 cm, or high grade & tumor >1 cm); MammaPrint low‑risk (MP1) confirmed (high‑risk/M P2 excluded). Biomarkers excluded: HER2‑positive status; MammaPrint high‑risk (MP2).
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Evidence of distant/metastatic disease.  
• Pathologic T4 tumours, including inflammatory breast cancer.  
• History of prior ipsilateral or contralateral invasive breast cancer (excluding prior DCIS or LCIS).  
• Prior ipsilateral DCIS treated with lumpectomy and radiation requiring mastectomy for current lesion.  
• Expected life expectancy <10 years owing to comorbidities.  
• Baseline laboratory abnormalities: absolute neutrophil count <1200/mm³, platelet count <100,000/mm³, hemoglobin <10 g/dL, serum bilirubin > upper limit of normal (or >1.5×ULN if related to benign hyperbilirubinemia), ALT or AST >3×institutional ULN, glomerular filtration rate <30 ml/min/1.73 m².  
• Cardiac impairment corresponding to NYHA functional classification poorer than class IIIB (class IIIA/B accepted).  
• Presence of non‑epithelial breast malignancies (sarcoma, lymphoma, etc.).  
• Receipt of any radiation therapy, cytotoxic chemotherapy, or immunobiologic therapy for the index breast cancer prior to enrolment (endocrine therapy >6 weeks also excluded).  
• Current use of hormonally based contraceptives (including progestin‑containing intrauterine devices).  
• Active chronic hepatitis B infection with detectable viral load despite therapy, or unresolved hepatitis C infection with detectable viral load.  
• Pregnant or breastfeeding at baseline (pregnancy test required within 2 weeks preceding enrolment).  
• Any other medical, psychiatric, or social circumstance judged by the investigators to compromise protocol adherence or data integrity.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: non squamous non small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non squamous. Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: no prior systemic therapy for advanced or metastatic disease; adjuvant or neoadjuvant therapy permissible if completed ≥6 months before diagnosis of advanced/metastatic disease and patient did not experience disease progression within 6 months after completion. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic therapy for advanced/metastatic non small cell lung cancer; any prior topoisomerase I–containing agent; any prior trop2‑targeted therapy; any prior anti PD‑1, anti PD‑L1, or anti PD‑L2 antibody; any prior agent directing other stimulatory or co inhibitory T‑cell receptors (e.g., ctla‑4, ox40, cd137); any other investigative immune checkpoint inhibitor. Biomarkers required: pd l1 tumour proportion score <50% as assessed centrally by icp 22c3 pharmdx; lack of actionable genomic alterations (absence of epidermal growth factor receptor sensitizing mutations, al k rearrangements, ros1 rearrangements, braf v600e mutation, met exon 14 skipping, ret fusions, ntrk fusions, or other FDA‑approved targeted-driver abnormalities). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease. Active uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including qtcf >470 ms, myocardial infarction ≤6 months, serious arrhythmias, uncontrolled angina ≤6 months, left ventricle ejection fraction <50%, nyha class ii‑iv heart failure not stabilized to class i with ef≥50%, uncontrolled hypertension (>180/>110 mmhg). Live vaccination within 30 days preceding first dose. Spinal cord compression or clinically active untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; newly detected brain metastases must demonstrate radiologic stability for ≥2 weeks off steroids. Severe pulmonary comorbidity such as recent pulmonary embolism (<3 months), severe asthma, severe copd, restrictive lung disease, extensive pleural effusion, autoimmune/connective tissue disease with lung involvement, or prior total pneumonectomy. Poor performance status (performance status >2). Hepatitis infection, hiv infection, or other uncontrolled infectious diseases. Renal impairment meeting protocol-defined thresholds (creatinine clearance <45 ml/min). Liver dysfunction exceeding protocol limits (bilirubin >1.5× upper limit normal or transaminases >3× ulN).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Any grade ≥ 2 peripheral neuropathy.
Active or historic autoimmune disease requiring systemic steroids or immunosuppressants (excluding vitiligo, resolved childhood asthma/atopy, isolated hypothyroidism, chronic skin conditions without systemic therapy, well‑controlled celiac disease).
Pregnancy or lactation.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness such as active infections, symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, significant cardiac arrhythmias, interstitial lung disease, severe GI disorder causing persistent diarrhoea, or psychiatric/social circumstances limiting compliance.
Mean QTcF ≥ 470 ms.
History of solid organ transplantation.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant cardiovascular disease within the past year. Uncontrolled seizure activity or severe neurocognitive impairment preventing compliance with study procedures. Involvement of the brain stem or spinal cord by tumor. Diffuse leptomeningeal dissemination evident on imaging. Ongoing chronic infection with human immunodeficiency virus, active hepatitis B or C infection. Baseline hepatic insufficiency (bilirubin > 1.5× upper limit of normal) or marked transaminase elevation (> 3× ULN). Renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min). Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status greater than 2. Concurrent malignant neoplasm requiring active therapy or relapse of a prior malignancy within less than three years (except adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer or carcinoma in situ). Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals. Any condition judged by the investigator to place the participant at undue risk or compromise data integrity.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: central nervous system astrocytoma. Histology allowed: grade 3 astrocytoma with high‑risk features. Cancer burden allowed: no evidence of disease progression after completing standard postoperative therapy. Prior treatment required: completion of external beam radiation therapy covering ≥90% of prescribed dose (may have included concurrent temozolomide) and administration of six to twelve cycles of adjuvant temozolomide with documented response; no subsequent disease progression. Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to anti‑angiogenic agents (e.g., bevacizumab), agents directly targeting IDH1 or IDH2, investigational glioma drugs, or ongoing tumor‑treating field therapy. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

HIV infection, chronic hepatitis B virus infection, or chronic hepatitis C virus infection  
Systemic glucocorticoid therapy beyond physiologic replacement, inhaled forms, or pre‑medication for transfusion  
Primary immunodeficiency or systemic autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression within the preceding 2 years  
Uncontrolled intercurrent medical illness  
Inability to comply with study requirements.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: Waldenström macroglobulinemia. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior chemotherapy directed at Waldenström macroglobulinemia, any Bruton’s Tyrosine Kinase inhibitor, any other systemic therapy for Waldenström macroglobulinemia, any prior anti‑CD20 antibody therapy (e.g., rituximab, ofatumumab). current enrollment in another therapeutic clinical trial; diagnosis of marginal zone lymphoma, chronic lymphocytic leukaemia, mantle cell lymphoma, IgM myeloma, or light chain (AL) amyloidosis; uncontrolled autoimmune haemolytic anaemia or idiopathic thrombocytopaenic purpura; gastrointestinal ulcer identified by endoscopy within three months preceding screening; known hypersensitivity, anaphylaxis, or intolerance to rituximab/ofatumumab or acalabrutinib; receipt of a live vaccine within four weeks before first rituximab dose; active malignancy within the past two years except cured basal/squamous skin cancer, cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, early breast cancer, or localized prostate cancer; any condition placing the participant at unreasonable risk; neuropathy due to causes other than IgM‑mediated mechanisms; concomitant use of moderate or strong CYP3A inhibitors/inducers within seven days of dosing; continuous daily proton‑pump inhibitor usage; decompensated cirrhosis (Child‑Pugh Class C); therapeutic anticoagulation with warfarin or equivalents; peripheral neuropathy duration greater than five years; documented central nervous system lymphoma; active clinically significant bleeding or inherited coagulopathy; serious cerebrovascular event (stroke/intracranial bleed) within six months; major surgical procedure within four weeks of starting study medication; malabsorptive syndromes preventing oral intake; pregnancy, lactation, or intention to conceive during study or shortly thereafter (female); intent to sire a child during study or within ninety days post‑treatment (male); uncontrolled cardiovascular disease—including symptomatic arrhythmias, NYHA class III/IV heart failure, or myocardial infarction within six months; active human immunodeficiency virus infection; active hepatitis B infection (positive HBsAg) or untreated hepatitis C infection (detectable HCV RNA); any acute infectious process at baseline; inability to swallow tablets; unresolved intercurrent illnesses compromising safety/compliance; unwillingness or inability to follow protocol procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: younger than 50 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: osteosarcoma. Histology allowed: osteosarcoma. Cancer burden allowed: up to four pulmonary nodules per lung, each measuring at least 3 mm and no greater than 3 cm, all judged resectable by open thoracotomy or video‑assist​ed thoracoscopic surgery; no extrapulmonary metastatic disease; no centrally located lesions necessitating lobectomy or pneumonectomy; no chest‑wall or mediastinal metastatic deposits; no clinically significant pleural effusion. Prior treatment required: gross total resection of the primary bone tumor (to be performed contemporaneously with thoracic surgery) and administration of standard front‑line multimodal chemotherapy containing cisplatin together with doxorubicin **or** an ifosfamide‑based regimen for newly diagnosed disease. general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients with unresectable primary tumors.
Pulmonary metastatic lesions requiring anatomic resection (lobectomy or pneumonectomy) or classified as central lesions near segmental bronchi.
Chest‑wall or mediastinal based metastatic lesions, or substantial pleural effusions.
Disease progression at the primary or pulmonary sites while on initial therapy.
Evidence of extrapulmonary metastatic disease.
Any therapeutic pulmonary surgery for lung metastases conducted prior to enrollment.
Incapacity to provide signed informed consent by patient or guardian.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Follicular lymphoma. Histology allowed: CD20 positive, grade 1, grade 2, or grade 3A. Prior treatment required: No prior systemic therapy for follicular lymphoma; prior localized radiation therapy or short‐course steroids allowable. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic anticancer therapy for follicular lymphoma (chemotherapy, targeted agents, monoclonal antibodies other than planned rituximab). Biomarkers required: Assessment of CD20 positivity performed on pretreatment tumor sample (required for eligibility). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients requiring chronic systemic immunosuppression (prednisone >10 mg/day) ; patients with bulky cervical adenopathy causing potential airway obstruction; candidates suitable for definitive curative radiation therapy; recent major surgery (<4 weeks) or unrecovered postoperative complications; active hepatitis B or C infection; past or present HIV infection; active serious bacterial, viral, fungal, mycobacterial, or parasitic infection at enrollment; prior malignancies other than non‑melanoma skin cancer or adequately treated carcinoma in situ unless disease-free ≥2 years; receipt of live attenuated vaccination within 1 week of dosing; uncontrolled concurrent medical illnesses affecting safety/compliance; recent (≤6 months) myocardial infarction, unstable angina, congestive heart failure NYHA class III–IV, left ventricular ejection fraction <45%, significant arrhythmias or electrocardiographic abnormalities, stroke, or peripheral arterial revascularisation; pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intention to conceive during study; prior solid organ or allogenic stem cell transplantation; history of haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis; active autoimmune disorders (unless remotely historic and well‑controlled after sponsor consult); severe hypersensitivity to anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibodies or components of epcoritamab; recent administration of live vaccines (within 28 days); central nervous system lymphoma; neurologic toxicity greater than grade 2; active COVID‑19 infection or unresolved suspicion of infection; abnormal baseline corrected QT interval >470 ms.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Estrogen receptor‑negative and progesterone receptor‑negative and HER2‑negative invasive carcinoma (triple‑negative). Cancer burden allowed: Residual invasive disease in the breast and/or lymph nodes after neoadjuvant chemotherapy (must have ≥2 mm invasive disease in an involved node if no residual invasive disease in the breast); presence of circulating tumor DNA in plasma confirmed by central testing. Prior treatment required: Completed neoadjuvant cytotoxic chemotherapy; definitive breast surgery with clear margins (lumpectomy or mastectomy) ± recommended whole‑breast irradiation for lumpectomy cases; axillary surgery as performed; bone‑modifying agents (bisphosphonates or RANK‑ligand inhibitors) may be used concurrently; prior immune checkpoint inhibitor administered in the neoadjuvant setting is permitted. Prior treatment excluded: Prior sacituzumab govitecan, irinotecan, or any topoisomerase I‑containing antibody‑drug conjugate for early‑stage disease at any time; any adjuvant (post‑surgical) systemic chemotherapy; documented hypersensitivity reaction to atezolizumab, sacituzumab govitecan, or their excipients. Biomarkers required: HER2 non‑amplified by in‑situ hybridisation (ratio < 2.0) **or** IHC 0 or 1+; estrogen receptor expression <10% of tumor cells by IHC; progesterone receptor expression <10% of tumor cells by IHC; circulating tumor DNA positive (detection of any tumor‑specific mutation by centralized assay). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Clinical or radiographic evidence of distant/metastatic disease; isolated residual ductal carcinoma in situ or lobular carcinoma in situ without invasive cancer; concurrent participation in another investigational therapy trial; unresolved treatment‑related adverse events greater than Grade 1 (excluding alopecia and peripheral neuropathy); major surgical procedures (other than diagnostic) within four weeks preceding study treatment or anticipated during the study; systemic antibacterial therapy within two weeks prior to starting study treatment (prophylaxis allowed); active infections needing systemic therapy, active tuberculosis, severe hepatic disease (cirrhosis), active bleeding disorders, uncontrolled diabetes mellitus, uncontrolled hyperlipidaemia, hypertension, hypertriglyceridaemia or hypercalcaemia; symptomatic congestive heart failure NYHA class III‑IV, recent myocardial infarction (< six months), unstable angina, serious arrhythmias or other significant cardiac disease; past or current idiopathic/pulmonary fibrotic syndromes, drug‑induced pneumonitis, or active pneumonitis on baseline imaging (radiation‐field fibrosis tolerated); active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy apart from controlled hypothyroidism, celiac disease, well‑controlled psoriasis, eczema, lichen simplex chronicus or vitiligo; gastrointestinal conditions impairing drug absorption (severe ulcerative colitis, uncontrolled nausea/vomiting/diarrhoea > Grade 2, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small‑bowel resections); congenital prolonged QT interval (>480 ms) or inherited long‑QT syndrome; HIV-positive, hepatitis B surface antigen‑positive, or hepatitis C RNA‑positive status; prior invasive breast cancer in either breast; other prior malignant tumors unless disease‑free for ≥ five years (thyroid cancer exempted if confined to gland without nodal spread); use of potent UGT1A1 enzyme inhibitors or inducers within four weeks or five elimination half‑lives before dosing; receipt of live attenuated vaccination within four weeks before initiating study treatment or planned thereafter; known allergic reactions to study drugs, their carriers, CHO‑cell derived components, or similar biologics; systemic immunosuppressive medications (high‑dose glucocorticoids, cyclophosphamide, azathioprine, methotrexate, thalidomide, anti‑TNFα agents) taken within two weeks before first dose (brief low‑dose pulses for allergy or COPD inhaler steroids permitted); pregnancy or breastfeeding; inability or unwillingness to use effective non‑hormonal contraception throughout treatment and for six months afterward.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Active uncontrolled or symptomatic central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.
Concurrent malignancy or malignancy diagnosed within 5 years (exceptions noted).
Known allergy or hypersensitivity to investigational product components.
Recent palliative radiotherapy (within 2–4 weeks depending on field size) or extensive bone marrow irradiation.
Major surgery or significant trauma within 14 days preceding study treatment.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease (unstable angina, recent MI, NYHA class II–IV CHF, poorly controlled hypertension, left ventricular ejection fraction <55%, symptomatic arrhythmias requiring intervention, prolonged corrected QT intervals).
Severe pulmonary impairment (recent pulmonary embolism, severe COPD/asthma, restrictive lung disease, fibrotic lung disease, prior pneumonectomy) or history/current/interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis.
Pregnancy, lactation, or inadequate contraception/use of ineffective contraception.
Active hepatitis B or C infection (unless meeting resolution criteria detailed in protocol).
Human immunodeficiency virus infection.
Other active uncontrolled infections.
Receipt of live or attenuated vaccination within 30 days prior to dosing.
History of uncontrolled seizures, ongoing central nervous system disorders, or disabling psychiatric illness impacting adherence.
Any other severe or uncontrolled medical condition deemed unsuitable by investigators.
Substance abuse or dependence potentially compromising safety/compliance.
Incapacity or unwillingness to abide by study assessments and follow‑up protocols.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukaemia. Histology allowed: acute myeloid leukaemia. Cancer burden allowed: high risk for post‑allohaematopoietic stem cell transplant relapse, defined as any of the following – de novo AML diagnosed at age ≥60 years (core‑binding factor AML excluded); de novo AML in first complete remission with meas­urable residual disease positive by flow cytometry; secondary AML; AML transplanted in second complete remission or thereafter; therapy‑related AML; TP53‑mutated AML (regardless of measurable residual disease). Prior treatment required: planned reduced‑intensity conditioning HLA‑matched related or related haploidentical allogeneic stem cell transplant with post‑cyclophosphamide graft‑vs‑host disease prophylaxis. Prior treatment excluded: use of sirolimus for GVHD prophylaxis; planned post‑transplant targeted maintenance therapy for FLT3‑ITD, IDH, or BCR‑ABL mutations; prior allogeneic stem cell transplant occurring ≤7 days before NK cell infusion; prior solid organ transplantation; prior grade ≥2 haemolytic anaemia; systemic glucocorticoids >10 mg prednisone equivalents on day of NK infusion. Biomarkers required: TP53 mutation (required only to waive measurable residual disease restriction). Biomarkers excluded: FLT3‑ITD mutation, IDH mutation, BCR‑ABL fusion when paired with planned targeted maintenance therapy; donor‑specific anti‑human leukocyte antigen antibodies with mean fluorescent intensity >1000 absent desensitisation protocol.

History of untreated or progressive central nervous system involvement (extramedullary leukemia in sanctuary sites such as CNS or testes).
Uncontrolled concurrent illnesses including active serious infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmias, or major psychiatric disorders limiting compliance.
Pregnancy or currently breastfeeding.
Positive HIV serostatus.
Active, uncontrolled hepatitis B or C infection.
Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin >1.5× ULN, ALT/AST >3× ULN) despite allowances noted for mild elevations.
Renal insufficiency with serum creatinine >2.0 mg/dL.
Performance status worse than ECOG 2/Karnofsky <60%.
Allergic reaction to components of interleukin‑2 or cellular therapeutic products.
Donor‑specific anti‑HLAB antibodies with mean fluorescence intensity >1000 lacking appropriate desensitisation protocol.

Prior treatment required: None. Prior treatment excluded: More than two prior systemic chemotherapy regimens. Biomarkers required: HER2 positivity (Immunohistochemistry 3⁺ **or** ISH amplified) (tested on archival or freshly obtained specimen). Biomarkers excluded: Estrogen‑receptor positive **or** progesterone‑receptor positive (not required for entry).
general_exclusion_criteria: History of symptomatic congestive heart failure Grade ≥2, NYHA Class ≥2, myocardium infarction, unstable angina; Left ventricular ejection fraction <50%; Prolonged corrected QT interval (>470 ms), complete left bundle branch block, Grade 3 AV block; Active autoimmune or inflammatory disorders requiring systemic therapy (excluding well‑controlled hypothyroidism, type I diabetes, mild skin disease); Additional malignancy diagnosed within past five years except adequately treated basal or squamous cell skin carcinoma or carcinoma in situ; Poorly controlled hypertension (SBP >150 mmHg or DBP >100 mmHg despite two antihypertensives); Interstitial lung disease or prior pneumonitis, Grade ≥3 respiratory disorder; Recent (<6 months) venous or arterial thromboembolic event needing therapeutic anticoagulation; Untreated or active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (treated/stable brain metastases permitted if off steroids >20 mg prednisolone equivalents for ≥14 days); Allergy to monoclonal antibodies or any component of the investigational agent; Previous hematopoietic stem‑cell transplant; Cumulative anthracycline exposure >360 mg/m²; Grade ≥2 hypokalaemia; Positive HIV antibody, active hepatitis B or C infection, active tuberculosis; Ongoing acute infectious process requiring systemic antibiotics; Pregnancy or lactation; Receipt of any other investigational medicinal product within four weeks preceding first dose.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast cancer. Cancer burden allowed: early-stage, surgically resected, no distant metastasis, high risk of recurrence according to prespecified clinicopathologic risk features, excluding inflammatory breast cancer. Prior treatment required: at least 24 months and not more than 60 months of any adjuvant endocrine therapy performed consecutively (no gap longer than 6 months) and continued until no more than 6 months before screening; prior (neoadjuvant or adjuvant) chemotherapy and/or targeted therapy with a CDK4/6 inhibitor or poly ADP ribose polymerase inhibitor permitted but not required. Prior treatment excluded: prophylactic endocrine therapy for breast cancer prevention (tamoxifen, aromatase inhibitors, raloxifene); prior endocrine therapy discontinued more than 6 months before screening; prior systemic therapy for any other malignant disease; prior distinct breast cancer event except ipsilateral ductal carcinoma in situ treated with local therapy ≥5 years ago. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor positive and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of metastatic disease (including contralateral axillary lymph node involvement).
Inflammatory breast cancer at initial diagnosis.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant or cause paternity during the study period.
Serious pre‑existing medical conditions deemed unsuitable by the investigator.
Performance status worse than ECOG 1.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: Multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: Multiple myeloma (plasmacytic neoplasm). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed and refractory disease with documented disease progression on or within 60 days of completing the most recent antimyeloma therapy. Prior treatment required: At minimum one prior line of therapy that included at least 2 cycles of lenalidomide **and** at least 2 cycles of a proteasome inhibitor. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Presence of measurable disease defined as serum monoclonal protein ≥0.5 g/dL **or** urinary monoclonal protein excretion ≥200 mg/24 hours **or** involved serum free light chain level ≥100 mg/L together with an abnormal serum free light chain ratio. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant cardiomyopathy, congestive heart failure New York Heart Association class III–IV, uncontrolled arterial hypertension, recent myocardial infarction or cerebrovascular accident (≤6 months), serious ventricular arrhythmias, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease requiring frequent hospitalization, interstitial lung disease or prior radiation-induced pneumonitis, active central nervous system involvement or untreated/uncontrolled brain metastasis, ongoing systemic infection (viral, bacterial, fungal, including HIV, hepatitis B or C active replication), unresolved toxicities greater than Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (excluding alopecia and stable peripheral neuropathy), concurrent enrollment in another interventional oncology trial, inability to tolerate oral medication administration, pregnant or breastfeeding individuals, inadequate organ function (renal impairment CrCl <30 mL/min, hepatic enzymes >2.5×upper limit of normal, bilirubin >1.5×ULN unless benign cause), platelet count <75 000/mm³, neutrophil count <1000/mm³, hemoglobin <8 g/dL, receipt of major surgical procedures within 28 days, recent live attenuated vaccinations within 30 days, and any condition judged by investigator to place the subject at unacceptable risk or compromise data integrity.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥55 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor (excluding prostate, breast, testicular, adrenal). Histology allowed: Any. Prior treatment required: Chemotherapy and/or radiation therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: (Total testosterone <348 ng/dL OR free testosterone <70 pg/mL) AND FACIT‑fatigue score <40. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of prostate, breast, testicular, or adrenal cancer.; Recent use of anabolic agents (testosterone, DHEA, growth hormone) within six months.; Hematocrit >48%; Serum creatinine >2.5 mg/dL.; PSA >4 ng/mL or palpable nodules/indurations on digital rectal exam.; Severe untreated sleep apnea.; Uncontrolled congestive heart failure.; myocardial infarction, acute coronary syndrome, revascularisation surgery, stroke or thromboembolism within six months.; Known hereditary thrombophilia (e.g., Factor V Leiden).; Prior stroke with lingering neurologic deficits.; Unable to give informed consent (Mini-Mental State Examination <24).; Poorly controlled diabetes (HbA1c >10%).; Body‑mass index >40 kg/m^2.; Diagnosis of bipolar disorder or schizophrenia.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Participants with hematologic lymphoma.
Patients with symptomatic central nervous system metastases requiring urgent radiation therapy or neurological instability.
History of severe hypersensitivity reaction to abemaciclib or chemically similar substances.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness such as active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, significant cardiac arrhythmia, or serious psychiatric disorder limiting compliance.
Pregnancy or lactation; women of child‑bearing potential must have a negative pregnancy test and use appropriate contraception.
Known HIV‐positive status.
Active chronic hepatitis B virus infection or hepatitis C virus infection.
Use of enzyme‑inducing anticonvulsant drugs that cannot be replaced before starting study therapy.

general_exclusion_criteria: Patients unable to undergo brain MRI; intracranial hemorrhage greater than grade 2; concomitant warfarin or vitamin K antagonist use within seven days of treatment start; arterial thromboembolic event such as stroke within three months; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression (prednisone >10 mg/day or equivalent) or treated within the past two years; necessity for high‑dose systemic corticosteroids (dex­ametasones >4 mg/day for over three consecutive days within two weeks of registration); unrecovered toxicities from prior therapy higher than grade 1; major surgical procedure within two weeks preceding treatment; incomplete recovery from prior radiation‑related toxicities or existing radiation pneumonitis; recent (<30 days) live vaccination; ongoing or progressive secondary malignancy requiring active treatment within three years (with exceptions for cured cutaneous carcinomas and carcinoma in situ); severe hypersensitivity reaction (grade ≥3) to study drugs or excipients; historical or current non‑infectious pneumonitis necessitating steroids; untreated active systemic infection graded >2 or recent IV antimicrobial therapy concluded less than fourteen days ago; clinically significant cardiovascular disease (uncontrolled/symptomatic arrhythmia, NYHA class III–IV heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within six months); uncontrolled hypertension despite optimized therapy; poorly controlled diabetes mellitus (HbA1c >8%) or steroid‑induced hyperglycemia meeting same threshold; non‑healing wounds, ulcers, or fractures; congenital or acquired bleeding diatheses (von Wilbrandt disease, hemophilia); inability to ingest oral capsule formulation or serious gastrointestinal malabsorption/resections; concurrent use of potent/moderate CYP3A4/5 inhibitors or inducers, including enzyme‑inducing anticonvulsants, without appropriate cessation; known human immunodeficiency virus infection; active hepatitis B (surface antigen positivity) or hepatitis C (detectable viral RNA); active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection; any condition, therapy, or lab abnormality compromising study integrity or participant safety; diagnosed immunodeficiency or chronic systemic glucocorticoid therapy exceeding 10 mg prednisone equivalents daily; prior allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation; unmanaged psychiatric illness or substance misuse impairing adherence; pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant or sire a child during the study period extending to 120 days after the last dose.

general_exclusion_criteria:
- Presence of plasma cell leukemic infiltration (≥5% circulating plasma cells)
- Diagnosis of Waldenström’s macroglobulinemia
- Diagnosis of POEMS syndrome
- Diagnosis of primary amyloid light‑chain amyloidosis
- Hypersensitivity or allergic reaction to any trial therapeutic or its components
- Completed spinal cord compression or central nervous system involvement
- Active or historical autoimmune disease requiring systemic immune suppression within two years (exceptions noted for controlled thyroid disease, Type 1 diabetes, vitiligo, psoriasis)
- Administration of a live, attenuated vaccine within four weeks preceding study drug initiation
- Ongoing graft‑versus‑host disease after allogeneic transplantation necessitating >20 mg/day prednisolone-equivalent glucocorticoids
- Uncontrolled active infections requiring antimicrobial therapy
- Any medical condition, concomitant therapy, or laboratory abnormality judged to potentially confound trial outcomes or impede completion of the study
- Significant psychiatric disorder or substance misuse compromising compliance
- Legal incapacity or lack of sufficient decision‑making authority
- Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Pregnancy or lactating individuals.
Known hypersensitivity to daratumumab‑hyaluronidase or any excipients.
Active untreated interstitial lung disease or recent pneumonitis.
Clinically significant cardiac disease including New York Heart Association class III–IV or reduced left ventricular ejection fraction.
Severe hepatic impairment (total bilirubin >2 mg/dL attributable to underlying liver disease) or transaminases >5 times upper limit of normal.
Renal impairment with creatinine clearance less than 30 mL/min.
Unresolved symptomatic central nervous system involvement or leptomeningeal disease.
Concurrent receipt of other antileukemic or experimental therapeutic agents.
Recent (within 28 days) administration of live vaccines.

general_exclusion_criteria: Currently participating in another interventional study or having received an investigational agent/device within 4 weeks of randomisation. Diagnosed immunodeficiency or receipt of systemic steroids or other immunosuppressants within 7 days prior to randomisation. Prior systemic antitumour therapy (investigational or approved) within 2 weeks of study Day 1. Prior radiotherapy within 2 weeks of starting study treatment. Presence of an additional actively progressing malignancy, excluding adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin carcinoma or carcinoma in situ. Active central nervous system metastases, leptomeningeal disease, or tumor involvement of the brain stem. Severe (grade ≥3) hypersensitivity reaction to pembrolizumab or its components. Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy within the preceding 2 years (hormone replacement alone permissible). History of non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring steroids or current pneumonitis. Uncontrolled active infection necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy. Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant or sire a child during the study period and until 120 days post‑last dose. Previous exposure to anti‑programmed cell death‑1, anti‑PD‑ligand 1, anti‑PD‑Ligand‑2, CTLA‑4, OX‑40, CD137 or related checkpoint modulators. Infection with human immunodeficiency virus, hepatitis B virus, or hepatitis C virus. Active tuberculosis infection. Receipt of a live attenuated vaccine within 30 days before initiating study drug. History of solid organ transplantation at any time, or allogenic haematopoietic stem‑cell transplantation within the past five years (transplant beyond five years allowable if graft-versus-host disease absent). Clinically significant medical, laboratory, or psychosocial conditions that, in the investigator’s judgment, would compromise safety or protocol compliance, including serious psychiatric illness or substance misuse.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Estrogen receptor positive and HER2 negative invasive carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Stage II to Stage III disease (AJCC 7th ed.) with no clinical evidence of local or distant recurrence and presence of disseminated tumor cells in bone marrow demonstrated by central immunohistochemistry. Prior treatment required: Completion of definitive breast surgery together with any prescribed adjuvant chemotherapy and radiation therapy; ongoing adjuvant endocrine therapy at enrollment (tamoxifen permissible only if switched to an aromatase inhibitor ≥21 days before study start for hydroxychloroquine arms); continued use of bone‑modifying agents (bisphosphonates or denosumab) allowed if already administered. Prior treatment excluded: Previous administration of palbociclib (any duration); concurrent enrollment in another interventional oncology trial; initiation of new bone‑modifying agents after study start. Biomarkers required: Detectable disseminated tumor cells in bone marrow by central IHC; confirmatory pathology showing estrogen receptor positivity and HER2 negativity on the primary tumor specimen. Biomarkers excluded: Tumors that are estrogen receptor negative and progesterone receptor positive; absence of detectable disseminated tumor cells in bone marrow.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of another prior invasive breast cancer (except contralateral DCIS diagnosed >5 years ago).
Active invasive non‑breast malignancies unless disease free ≥5 years.
Chronic high‑dose systemic corticosteroid use (>specified doses) or other immunosuppressive agents.
QTc prolongation > 480 ms on ECG.
Uncontrolled serious comorbidities (severe/uncontrolled auto­immune disease, uncontrolled arrhythmia except stable atrial fibrillation, recent (<6 months) acute coronary events, NYHA class II or greater heart failure, recent stroke, uncontrolled hypertension).
Recent major cardiovascular interventions (angioplasty/stent placement) within past 6 months.
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease, DLCO <50 % predicted, or resting oxygen sat ≤88 % on ambient air.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus.
Active severe bacterial, viral, fungal, or opportunistic infection.
Significant liver disease (cirrhosis, chronic active/hepatitis B/C).
HIV infection requiring combination antiretroviral therapy.
Gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral drug absorption (ulcerative colitis, Crohn's disease, untreated nausea/vomiting/diarrhea, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small‑bowel resections).
Bleeding diatheses or requirement for therapeutic anticoagulation.
Retinal disease history (retinopathy, retinal vein occlusion).
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or lack of adequate contraception in persons of childbearing potential.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: squamous. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic (stage IV). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic therapy for metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer. Biomarkers required: PD‑L1 tumour proportion score >50% (22C3 IHC), assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: any known actionable genomic alteration for which a first‑line targeted therapy is approved.
general_exclusion_criteria: Presence of small cell lung carcinoma histology. Receipt of any prior therapy for non‑small cell lung cancer in the metastatic setting. Enrollment in another interventional clinical study concurrently. Known actionable genomic alterations for which first‑line approved therapies exist. Symptomatic central nervous system metastases, CNS lesions ≥1.5 cm, CNS radiation within 7 days prior to randomisation, planned CNS radiation within the first treatment cycle, or leptomeningeal disease. Other prior malignant neoplasm unless definitive cure achieved with no evidence of disease for at least three years. Active autoimmune disorder or pulmonary disease requiring systemic therapy. Peripheral neuropathy graded ≥2. Serious infection occurring within four weeks preceding randomisation. Major surgical operation or significant traumatic injury within four weeks preceding randomisation. History of non‑infectious pneumonia necessitating systemic glucocorticoids or existing interstitial lung disease.

general_exclusion_criteria:
- Uncontrolled cardiac disease including congestive heart failure classified as New York Heart Association class III or greater, recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, significant arrhythmias (ventricular tachyarrhythmia, ventricular fibrillation) or documented syncope of cardiac cause, or prior sudden cardiac arrest.
- Significant respiratory illness such as severe pulmonary disease, interstitial lung disease, or resting hypoxia requiring supplemental oxygen.
- Active systemic infectious processes requiring IV antimicrobial therapy, including bacterial sepsis, opportunistic fungi, or clinically evident viral infections (HIV positivity, active hepatitis B or C).
- Moderate-to-severe hepatic impairment (AST or ALT >5× upper limit of normal, total bilirubin >2× ULN) or renal insufficiency with estimated glomerular filtration rate less than 30 mL/min.
- Untreated or symptomatic central nervous system involvement, particularly uncontrolled brain metastasis.
- Pre-existing gastrointestinal disorders expected to result in baseline grade 2 or worse diarrhea (Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis, extensive intestinal resections).
- Any concomitant serious comorbidity judged by the investigator to jeopardize safety or protocol compliance.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Current or past allergic reaction attributable to peposertib, M1774, or substances of comparable structure.  
• Participation in another concurrently administered investigational drug protocol.  
• Unresolved clinically significant toxicities greater than Grade 1 from preceding anticancer therapy (excluding alopecia, adequately managed endocrinopathies, or skin toxicity up to Grade 2).  
• Clinically active, uncontrolled central nervous system involvement (brain metastasis or leptomeningeal disease) deemed to require immediate local therapy.  
• Significant uncontrolled comorbidity, including active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, life‑threatening cardiac arrhythmia, or other acute medical conditions compromising safe study conduct.  
• Baseline corrected QT interval (QTcF) exceeding 470 milliseconds.  
• Women who are pregnant or nursing, and men/couples unable to comply with mandated contraceptive precautions.  
• Inability to safely discontinue concomitant medicines or dietary supplements identified as potent inducers or inhibitors of key pharmacokinetic pathways (CYP3A4/5, CYP2C19, CYP2C9, P‑gp, BCRP, OCT1, OATP1B1/OATP1B3) when appropriate alternatives are unavailable.

Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression exceeding physiologic glucocorticoid levels within 2 weeks before screening.
Unstable cardiovascular disease—including unstable angina, myocardial infarction ≤6 months ago, or left ventricular ejection fraction <45%.
Concurrent malignancies (with few predefined exceptions).
Serious uncontrolled medical illnesses (active uncontrolled infections, uncontrolled HIV, hepatitis B or C without meeting suppression criteria, severe pulmonary, hepatic, or renal impairment).
Requirement for chronic supplemental oxygen to maintain saturations.
Clinical evidence of dementia or markedly altered mental status.
Major surgical procedure performed ≤4 weeks before leukapheresis or anticipated ≤4 weeks post‑infusion.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant/breed within 12 months following infusion.
Known allergy or hypersensitivity to study agent constituents (dimethyl sulfoxide) or to lymphodepleting regimen drugs (fludarabine, cyclophosphamide) or to tocilizumab.
Residual grade ≥2 toxicity attributable to preceding anticancer therapy persisting at baseline (alopecia exempt).
Previous receipt of allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation or solid organ transplantation.

Previous receipt of allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation or solid organ transplantation.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Receipt of investigational anticancer agent within 5 half‑lives prior to study entry (except ongoing BTK inhibitor).
* Major surgical procedure within 4 weeks preceding study start.
* Radiation therapy within 7 days of starting study treatment.
* Active hepatitis B infection (HBsAg positive) or active hepatitis C infection (positive HCV PCR).
* Uncontrolled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection (unless meeting immune and virologic thresholds).
* Other active malignancies requiring therapy within the past 3 years (with noted exceptions).
* Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or unwillingness to comply with contraception/REMS requirements.
* Current serious infectious process needing intravenous antimicrobial therapy or recent hospitalization for infection (<4 weeks).
* Significant preexisting liver disease or uncontrolled alcoholic liver injury.
* Central nervous system involvement by lymphoma (parenchymal or leptomeningeal).
* Grade ≥ 2 adverse effects attributable to prior cancer therapies.
* Severe hypersensitivity/allergic reaction to monoclonal antibodies or related biologics.
* History of Progressive Multifocal Leukoencephalopathy.
* Recent use (outside washout considerations) of systemic immunosuppressants or steroids exceeding stipulated limits.
* Autologous or allogeneic stem cell transplant within 3 months of enrolment (without requisite hematologic recovery).
* High risk for thromboembolic events refusing anticoagulation prophylaxis.
* Ongoing participation in other anticancer or experimental therapeutics.
* Live vaccination within 28 days before initiating study drugs.
* Unstable cardiovascular disease (e.g., NYHA class III–IV, recent MI, uncontrolled CHF, arrhythmias) or severe pulmonary disease.

Significant cardiovascular disease within 6 months (recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, coronary interventions, uncontrolled arrhythmias, thromboembolic/cerebrovascular event, or prolonged QT interval >470 ms). Any other active malignancy within 3 years except adequately treated basal cell carcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma, or carcinoma in situ. Ongoing grade ≥2 peripheral sensory or motor neuropathy. History of Guillain‑Barre syndrome or grade ≥3 peripheral motor polyneuropathy. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Known hypersensitivity to elranatamab or its excipients. Concurrent investigational agents for multiple myeloma. Receipt of live attenuated vaccine within 4 weeks of first study dose. Unresolved toxicities higher than grade 1 (excluding alopecia and stable peripheral neuropathy). Major surgery or invasive procedures within 14 days. Serious psychiatric disorder or recent suicidality. Administration of any investigational drug within 30 days or five half‑lifes periods prior to study drug initiation.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Major surgery within 14 days prior to receiving study drug or not recovered from major postoperative complications.
Use of prohibited concomitant medications that cannot be stopped 7 days before study drug initiation.
Receipt of other investigational therapeutic agents.
Administration of additional anticancer agents apart from study medication during the trial.
Co‑medications that may interfere with study outcomes (e.g., systemic immunosuppressants other than permissible corticosteroids).
Herbal medicines potentially interacting with study drug pharmacokinetics.
Known hypersensitivity to any components of abemaciclib formulation.
Active systemic bacterial infection requiring IV antibiotics, active fungal infection, or clinically evident viral infection (including HIV positivity, active Hepatitis B or C).
Significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing drug absorption (ulcerative disease, uncontrolled N/V/D, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small‑bowel resection).
Cardiovascular history of unexplained syncopal episodes, pathologic ventricular arrhythmias, or prior sudden cardiac death.
Severe or uncontrolled comorbidities posing undue safety risk (e.g., chronic pancreatitis, uncontrolled hepatic disease).
Incapacity to ingest oral tablets.
Serious unmanaged pulmonary conditions (interstitial lung disease, severe resting dyspnoea, dependence on supplemental O2).
Renal insufficiency with calculated creatinine clearance <30 mL/min.
History of major gastrectomy or substantial small‑bowel resection affecting nutrition/drug uptake.
Pre-existing inflammatory bowel disease (Crohn’s disease or ulcerative colitis) or chronic condition leading to baseline grade 2 or greater diarrhoea.
Documented non‑adherence/compliance issues with prescribed regimens.
Any second malignant neoplasm diagnosed within the past two years with current activity, excluding adequately treated basal or squamous cell skin carcinomas, non‑melanoma skin cancer, or surgically cured cervical cancer.

general_exclusion_criteria:

Pathological complete response after neoadjuvant therapy.
History of any other malignancy except non‑melanoma skin cancer or cervical intraepithelial neoplasia grade I, unless in complete remission off therapy for ≥5 years.
Severe or uncontrolled systemic illnesses compromising safety/compliance.
Left ventricular ejection fraction < 50% with New York Heart Association Class II or higher heart failure.
Mean resting corrected QT interval > 480 milliseconds at screening.
Concurrent exogenous reproductive hormone therapy or non‑topical hormonal therapy for non‑cancer conditions.
Any concurrent anti‑cancer treatment not stipulated in the protocol (excluding bisphosphonates or RANKL inhibitors).
Previous receipt of camizestrant, investigational SERDs/E​R‑targeting agents, or fulvestrant.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Known hypersensitivity to camizestrant, its excipients, or chemically similar compounds.
Hypersensitivity/intolerance to LHRH agonists in pre/peri‑menopausal females or males requiring their use.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients who have not fully recovered from prior anticancer therapy‐related adverse events beyond Grade 1 (except alopecia).
Concurrent receipt of any other investigational agents.
History of hypersensitivity reaction to substances chemically or biologically similar to tivozanib or nivolumab.
Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic ≥151 mm Hg or diastolic ≥101 mm Hg despite optimal medication).
Active autoimmune disorder requiring systemic therapy, except limited cases (vitiligo, alopecia, stable hypothyroidism, mild chronic dermatologic conditions).
Active HIV infection not suppressed (detectable viral load) or not on antiretrovirals.
Significant coexisting medical, psychiatric, or laboratory abnormalities judged likely to compromise safety/compliance.
Systemic glucocorticoid usage exceeding prednisolone ≤10 mg/day (or equivalent).
Pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraception adherence.
Concomitant strong cytochrome P450 3A enzyme inducing medicines.

general_exclusion_criteria: Laboratory values outside allowable ranges (creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min; total bilirubin > 1.5× upper limit of normal [> 3.0× ULN if Gilberts]; alanine aminotransferase or aspartate aminotransferase > 3× ULN; absolute neutrophil count < 1.0 x 10⁹/L; platelet count < 75 x 10⁹/L; hemoglobin < 9 g/dL). Significant cardiovascular disease (uncontrolled heart failure NYHA grade ≥ II, uncontrolled hypertension, serious arrhythmias, QTcF > 470 ms, acute myocardial infarction or unstable angina within 3 months). Active coronavirus disease 2019 infection. Inability or refusal to ingest oral capsule formulation.
