general_exclusion_criteria:
Unable to swallow oral medication.
Pregnant or breastfeeding.
Concurrent participation in another interventional study.
Leptomeningebral disease.
Known coagulation disorder.
History of another primary cancer, except cured non‑melanoma skin cancer or cured carcinoma in situ, with other past malignancies required to be disease‑free for ≥3 years.
Presence of replication‑repair‑deficient glioma (e.g., constitutional mismatch repair deficiency or Lynch syndrome).
Hypersensitivity to any component of vorasidenib or temozolomide.
Significant active cardiac disease within six months (New York Heart Association class III or IV heart failure, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, stroke).
Resting QTcF ≥450 ms or other risk factors for torsades de pointes.
Active hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection, or untreated/HIV infection (controlled/chronic infection permissible per protocol).

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of active central nervous system involvement with acute myeloid leukemia.
Diagnosis of acute promyelocytic leukemia, inversion 16 AML, or t(8;21) AML.
New York Heart Association functional classification II or higher (cardiac disability).
Baseline corrected QT interval (Fridericia) > 450 msec or concomitant use of QT‑prolonging drugs.
Positive human immunodeficiency virus infection.
Active hepatitis B or C infection (unless undetectable viral load within past three months).
Significant gastrointestinal malabsorption syndromes impairing oral medication absorption.
Chronic respiratory disease requiring continuous supplemental oxygen.
Uncontrolled systemic infection or other medically unstable condition judged to compromise safety.
Severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance < 60 ml/min) or hepatic insufficiency (AST/ALT > 2.5 times upper limit normal, total bilirubin > 1.5 times upper limit normal unless attributable to leukemic infiltration).

general_exclusion_criteria: Patients must have fully recovered from Grade ≤1 residual toxicities of prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia); ongoing severe adverse events from prior therapy preclude enrolment. Concurrent participation in another investigational study or receipt of other investigational agents prohibited. Known hypersensitivity to APG‑1252, cobimetinib, or structurally related compounds excludes participant. Current use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducer drugs (unless stopped ≥7 days before study start) disqualifies. Uncontrolled comorbid conditions—including unstable cardiovascular disease, congestive heart failure NYHA Class III-IV, uncontrolled hypertension, arrhythmias, recent myocardial infarction, serious infections, untreated HIV/HBV/HCV with detectable viremia, hepatic impairment >Grade 2, renal insufficiency GFR <50 ml/min, or baseline ECOG Performance Status >2—not permitted. Active or progressive CNS metastasis, including leptomeningeal disease, excludes subject. Significant ocular pathology (retinal vascular occlusions, neovascular macular degeneration, retinitis pigmentosa, etc.) contraindicates enrolment. Pregnancy or lactation leads to immediate exclusion; reliable contraceptive use required throughout study and for 2 weeks post‑treatment. Prior exposure to BCL‐family inhibitors bars participation. Gastrointestinal diseases compromising oral medication absorption (e.g., malabsorptive syndromes, extensive surgical resections, obstructive episodes within 90 days) or reliance on total parental nutrition render patient ineligible.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Epithelial ovarian cancer, Fallopian tube cancer, Primary peritoneal cancer. Histology allowed: Low‑grade serous carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Recurrent disease after at least one prior platinum‑containing systemic therapy; disease must be platinum‑resistant (progression within 6 months of last platinum). Prior treatment required: Minimum one prior line of platinum‑based chemotherapy **AND** prior MEK inhibitor administered at a therapeutically active dose (trametinib ≥1 mg once daily OR binimetinib ≥30 mg twice daily) **AND**, if previously exposed to cobimetinib, tolerability at planned study dose/schedule. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients must have fully recovered from Grade ≤1 residual toxicities of prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia); ongoing severe adverse events from prior therapy preclude enrolment. Concurrent participation in another investigational study or receipt of other investigational agents prohibited. Known hypersensitivity to APG‑1252, cobimetinib, or structurally related compounds excludes participant. Current use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducer drugs (unless stopped ≥7 days before study start) disqualifies. Uncontrolled comorbid conditions—including unstable cardiovascular disease, congestive heart failure NYHA Class III-IV, uncontrolled hypertension, arrhythmias, recent myocardial infarction, serious infections, untreated HIV/HBV/HCV with detectable viremia, hepatic impairment >Grade 2, renal insufficiency GFR <50 ml/min, or baseline ECOG Performance Status >2—not permitted. Active or progressive CNS metastasis, including leptomeningeal disease, excludes subject. Significant ocular pathology (retinal vascular occlusions, neovascular macular degeneration, retinitis pigmentosa, etc.) contraindicates enrolment. Pregnancy or lactation leads to immediate exclusion; reliable contraceptive use required throughout study and for 2 weeks post‑treatment. Prior exposure to BCL‐family inhibitors bars participation. Gastrointestinal diseases compromising oral medication absorption (e.g., malabsorptive syndromes, extensive surgical resections, obstructive episodes within 90 days) or reliance on total parental nutrition render patient ineligible.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 11–50 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Testicular germ cell tumor. Histology allowed: Seminoma. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease; intermediate or poor risk per International Germ Cell Consensus Classification. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Extensive prior chemotherapy or radiotherapy (exception: permissive low‑dose induction regimens as described). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of another primary malignancy (except adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, germ cell tumor, or malignancies treated ≥5 years ago with no recurrence).
Significant cardiac disease precluding adequate intravenous hydration for cisplatin administration.
Severe chronic respiratory disease that contraindicates use of bleomycin.
Peripheral neuropathy grade ≥ 2 or clinically significant sensorineural hearing loss/tinnitus.
Active severe infections or other acute illnesses compromising safe participation.
Inability to use appropriate contraception (men must employ dual method contraception throughout treatment and for one year thereafter).
Known allergic reaction or hypersensitivity to any study medication components.
Psychiatric, social, geographic, or substance‑use issues judged by investigators to impede adherence to protocol requirements.

general_exclusion_criteria: Subjects with neuroblastoma whose disease is confined solely to bone marrow evaluation; major surgical procedure performed within 14 days before first dose; active bacterial, fungal, or viral infection—including human immunodeficiency virus positivity; gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral drug absorption such as Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis, or short bowel syndrome; baseline electrocardiographic findings meeting any of the following: corrected QT interval >480 msec, clinically significant rhythm or conduction abnormality, or conditions increasing risk of torsades de pointe (heart failure, congenital long QT syndrome, familial long QT syndrome, or concomitant medications that lengthen QT); peripheral neuropathy graded ≥2; current or anticipated coadministration of potent cytochrome P450 3A4 inhibitors or inducers; documented hypersensitivity to repotrectinib or any of its formulation components.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Active central nervous system tumors or metastases.  
* Concurrent anticancer therapy (chemotherapy, radiotherapy [except palliative local RT with sponsor approval], hormonal therapy, biologics, or immunotherapy) or investigational agent administration within five drug half‑lives or fourteen days prior to study start.  
* Ongoing systemic steroids >10 mg/day prednisolone-equivalent or other immunosuppressive drugs.  
* Planned or recent (<30 days) live attenuated vaccination.  
* Unresolved grade ≥ 2 adverse events from prior cancer therapy (excluding alopecia, vitiligo, and predefined lab abnormalities).  
* Pregnancy, lactation, or inadequate contraceptive measures for women of child‑bearing potential; refusal to use condoms/sperm donation restrictions for sexually active participants.  
* Clinically significant comorbidities compromising safety or adherence (e.g., uncontrolled infections, active interstitial lung disease, severe cardiovascular disease, hepatic impairment, renal insufficiency, uncontrolled diabetes, active GI bleed, coagulopathy, unmanaged psychiatric disorders).  
* Enrollment in another interventional clinical trial concurrently.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Infiltrating breast carcinoma. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically evident pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease, or findings consistent with pneumonitis/ILD on baseline imaging.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses such as uncontrolled hypertension, active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, serious cardiac arrhythmias, or severe psychiatric disorder limiting adherence.
Active or untreated hepatitis B/C infection (unless meeting antiviral control criteria detailed in protocol).
Well‑controlled HIV infection requires CD4⁺ ≥ 350 cells/mm³ and sustained viral suppression; however, uncontrolled HIV remains an exclusion.
QTcF prolongation greater than 470 milliseconds on screening electrocardiogram.
Previous diagnosis of myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukaemia, or signs suggesting evolving MDS/AML.
Allergy or hypersensitivity reaction attributable to axatilimab, olaparib, or structurally related compounds.
Concurrent administration of strong or moderate cytochrome P450 (especially CYP3A) inhibitors or inducers.
Inability to safely ingest oral medication or presence of gastrointestinal conditions impairing drug absorption (malabsorption syndromes, extensive gastric/bowel resection).
Recent major surgical procedures within 28 days preceding study enrollment.
Clinical progression on a poly(ADP‑ribose) polymerase inhibitor, or exposure to a PARP inhibitor within the preceding twelve months.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of interstitial lung disease, idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organising pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, or active pneumonitis on imaging. History of bowel perforation or intestinal fistula within 6 months. Unable to swallow oral medication or malabsorption secondary to gastrectomy or active inflammatory bowel disease. Active dermatological condition requiring systemic therapy within past year. History of rhabdomyolysis. Significant ophthalmologic diseases including glaucoma, retinal vein occlusion, elevated intra‑ocular pressure >21 mmHg, corneal epithelial instability, severe keratitis, or other serious ocular surface inflammation. Hypersensitivity to study drugs. Previous allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant or solid organ transplant. Untreated central nervous system metastasis; CNS mets must have been locally treated, steroid free, and stable ≥1 month. Residual Grade ≥2 toxicities from prior anticancer therapy (except Grade 2 alopecia). Uncontrolled HIV, Hepatitis B (unless suppressed) or Hepatitis C (unless cured or virologically suppressed). Left ventricular ejection fraction <50%. Concurrent strong CYP3A4 inducer/inhibitor usage unable to stop appropriately. Recent exposure (<14 days) to P‑glycoprotein modulators. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Poor performance status (ECOG ≥2). Severe cytopenias (ANC <1500/mm³, platelets <100 k/µL, hemoglobin ≤9 g/dL). Impaired hepatic function (bilirubin >1.5×ULN [≤3 mg/dL if Gilberts], AST/ALT >2.5×ULN). Renal impairment (serum creatinine >1.5×ULN or CrCl <30 mL/min). Elevated CK (>2.5×ULN).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Systemic light chain amyloidosis (AL amyloidosis). Histology allowed: Light chain amyloid deposits confirmed by Congo red stain with apple‑green birefringence. Cancer burden allowed: Measurable disease (serum M‑spike ≥ 500 mg/dL or serum free light chain ≥ 20 mg/L with abnormal κ/λ ratio or differential free light chain ≥ 20 mg/L); plasma cell infiltrates ≤60% in bone marrow; no osteolytic bone lesions or end‑organ damage characteristic of overt multiple myeloma; cardiac involvement classified as Mayo stage I, II, or IIIa per 2013 European modification (NT‑ProBNP ≤ 8500 pg/mL together with applicable troponin thresholds). Prior treatment required: At least one prior line of anti‑plasma‑cell therapy (any regimen). Prior treatment excluded: Prior exposure to ixazomib (MLN9708) or venetoclax. Biomarkers required: Presence of t(11;14) translocation detected by fluorescence in‑situ hybridisation on bone‑marrow sample (confirmed at screening or documented previously). Biomarkers excluded: Absence of t(11;14) translocation.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of significant cardiovascular disease such as uncontrolled hypertension, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, recent myocardial infarction (within 6 months), or NYHA class III/IV symptoms.
Active central nervous system involvement of disease.
Uncontrolled infectious diseases, including active bacterial, fungal, tuberculosis, untreated hepatitis B/C, or HIV infection not adequately controlled.
Severe hepatic impairment (bilirubin >1.5× ULN, AST/ALT >3× ULN).
Renal insufficiency with creatinine clearance <15 mL/min.
Peripheral neuropathy grade ≥ 3 or grade 2 with painful exam finding.
Grade ≥ 3 diarrhoea.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Inadequate hematologic parameters (leukocytes <3000/mm³, ANC <1000/mm³, platelets <75000/mm³) despite prohibited supportive measures.
Significant concomitant illnesses or laboratory abnormalities judged by investigator to preclude safe participation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. general_exclusion_criteria: History of myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukaemia; any known predisposition to bleeding; persistent severe cytopenia; severe or uncontrolled systemic disease or active uncontrolled infection; refractory nausea/vomiting, chronic gastrointestinal disease, inability to swallow formulation, or prior extensive bowel resection; history of another primary malignancy; persistent CTCAE grade ≥ 2 toxicities from prior anti‑cancer therapy (excluding alopecia); spinal cord compression, brain metastases, carcinomatous meningitis, or leptomeningeal disease; active and uncontrolled hepatitis B or hepatitis C; active and uncontrolled HIV infection; active tuberculosis infection; cardiac abnormalities including arrhythmia or cardiovascular disease; concurrent exogenous reproductive hormone therapy or non‑topical hormonal therapy for non‑cancer indications; major surgical procedure or significant trauma within four weeks of first dose or expected need for major surgery during study; palliative radiotherapy involving a limited field within two weeks or broad field/widespread bone‐marrow irradiation within four weeks before first dose; prior systemic anti‑cancer treatment for locoregional recurrence or metastatic disease outside allowable endocrine therapy; receipt of blood products or growth factors within twenty‑eight days preceding randomisation; any concurrent systemic anti‑cancer therapy; use of strong/moderate CYP3A4 inducers or inhibitors, sensitive CYP2B6 substrates, narrow‑therapeutic‑index CYP2C9/CYP2C19 substrates within appropriate clearance periods; drugs known to prolong QT/QT‑interval and increase torsades de pointes risk; systemic atropine administration.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of uncontrolled brain metastasis or brain lesions progressing despite treatment. Recent extensive radiotherapy (extended-field ≤4 weeks or palliative ≤2 weeks) without full recovery. Active infection requiring intravenous or oral antibiotic therapy at enrolment. Serious uncontrolled medical disorders compromising safety or compliance. Psychiatric or social conditions limiting adherence to trial procedures. Presence of an active second malignant neoplasm, except stable prostate cancer under observation, in situ cervical/breast carcinomas, or localized non‑melanoma skin cancer. Uncontrolled or significant cardiovascular disease classified as New York Heart Association class III or IV. Baseline corrected QT interval >470 ms (Fridericia method) in patients without a pacemaker. Autoimmune diseases necessitating chronic systemic corticosteroids exceeding 10 mg daily prednisone equivalents (excluding isolated vitiligo, Type I diabetes, Graves’ disease, or Hashimoto’s thyroiditis). Discontinuation of any prior immune‑checkpoint inhibitor owing to immune‑related adverse events that have not resolved to grade 1 or lower or continue to require >10 mg prednisone-equivalent daily. Past pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease of any etiology. Receipt of live vaccinations within 30 days preceding registration. Documented active hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection. Positive HIV infection meeting special entry criteria (requires CD4⁺ ≥ 350 cells/mm³, suppressed viral load <400 copies/ml, ART ≥4 weeks, no opportunistic infections); otherwise excluded. Known hypersensitivity to investigational product, formulation components, or associated devices.

Evidence of tumor not amenable to minimally invasive resection on pre‑operative imaging (including persistent ascites, small bowel/gastric involvement, colon or rectal involvement, diaphragmatic involvement, splenic or hepatic surface/parenchymal involvement, mesenteric involvement, tumor infiltration of the lesser peritoneal sac).
History of psychological, familial, sociological or geographic conditions potentially preventing compliance with the study protocol and follow‑up schedule.
Inability to tolerate prolonged Trendelenburg positioning or pneumoperitoneum as determined by investigators.
Any other contraindications to minimally invasive surgery as judged by the clinician.

general_exclusion_criteria: Untreated or clinically symptomatic central nervous system metastases and/or leptomeningeal disease. Major surgery performed within 14 days preceding enrolment. Serious or uncontrolled pre‑existent medical conditions such as interstitial lung disease, severe resting dyspnea or dependence on supplemental oxygen, severe renal insufficiency, extensive gastric or small‑bowel resections, inflammatory bowel disease causing baseline Grade 2 diarrhoea or worse. Gastrointestinal disorders likely to impair absorption of oral medication (ulcerative disease, uncontrolled nausea/vomiting/diarrhea, malabsorptive syndromes, substantial small‑bowel resection). Active systemic bacterial infection requiring IV antibiotics, invasive fungal infection, or documented viral infection (positive HIV serostatus, active hepatitis B or C). Significant cardiac history including syncopal episodes of cardiovascular origin, pathogenic ventricular arrhythmias (ventricular tachycardia, ventricular fibrillation) or witnessed sudden cardiac death. Pregnancy or current lactation. Use of vitamin K antagonists requiring regular International Normalized Ratio monitoring (e.g., warfarin). Receipt of any investigational agent within the past 30 days or five drug half‑lives, or simultaneous participation in another incompatible clinical study. Prolonged corrected QT interval on ECG greater than 450 ms in males or greater than 470 ms in females.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Presence of another concomitant malignancy unless definitive curative therapy was undertaken with no evidence of disease for at least three years.
* Unstable central nervous system metastases, including leptomeningeal involvement or radiographic progression within four weeks.
* Administration of a live or live‑attenuated vaccine within thirty days prior to the first dose of study medication.
* Exposure to an investigative medicinal product or device within four weeks prior to study treatment.
* Diagnosis of immunodeficiency or receipt of chronic systemic corticosteroid or other immunosuppressive therapy within seven days prior to the first dose (physiological replacement steroids permitted; inhaled or topical steroids allowed when no active auto­immune disease is present).
* Active keratitis or corneal ulceration (treated superficial punctate keratitis permitted).
* Active autoimmune disease that required systemic therapeutic intervention within the past two years, aside from hormone replacement therapy.
* History of uncontrolled diabetes mellitus.
* Past or current non‑infectious pneumonitis that required systemic steroids.
* Ongoing infectious process (viral, bacterial, or fungal) mandating systemic antimicrobial therapy.
* Confirmed human immunodeficiency virus infection.
* Chronic hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection.
* Major surgical operation performed within four weeks before the initial dose of study drug.
* Previous allogeneic solid organ or hematopoietic stem‑cell transplantation.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Active leptomeningeal disease or symptomatic spinal cord compression.
Uncontrolled pleural effusion, pericardial effusion, or ascites requiring frequent drainage.
History of any other malignancy diagnosed within two years preceding enrollment, excluding the index cancer being studied and cured localized recurrence.
Significant impairment of pulmonary function.
Clinically significant ongoing infections.
Serologic positivity for active hepatitis B virus or hepatitis C virus infection.
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection (treated HIV infection permissible only in Phase 1b meeting extra criteria).
High cardiovascular risk profile.
QTc interval greater than 470 milliseconds on screening electrocardiogram or additional risk factors for torsades de pointes.
Residual toxicity from prior anti‑cancer therapies that has not returned to baseline or stabilised.
Conditions preventing oral intake or causing major gastrointestinal functional compromise.
Pregnancy or lactation in female participants.
Concurrent enrolment in another therapeutic interventional clinical study (observational/non‑interventional studies allowable).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia (mature B‑cell neoplasm). Cancer burden allowed: measurably elevated disease defined as absolute lymphocyte count >10 ×10⁹/L or total white blood cell count ≥20 ×10⁹/L; no bulk adenopathy greater than 5 cm; fulfills International Workshop on CLL 2018 criteria for being treatment‑naïve and has no indication for initiating therapy; lacks chromosome 17p deletion in ≥10% of cells; no Richter transformation; lymphocyte‑doubling time ≥6 months if white blood cell count >30 ×10³/µL; serum immunoglobulin concentration ≥400 mg/dL without chronic intravenous immunoglobulin support; no untreated autoimmune cytopenia. Prior treatment required: none (patient must be treatment‑naïve). Prior treatment excluded: any prior CLL‑directed therapy (chemotherapy, targeted agents, Bruton tyrosine kinase inhibitor, Bcl‑2 antagonist, anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody, etc.), prior anti‑programmed death‑1/programmed death ligand‑1/programmed death ligand‑2 therapy, prior hematopoietic stem cell or bone‑marrow transplant, prior therapeutic radiation or hormonally acting oncology drugs administered within the preceding 2 years, participation in another investigational anticancer study, chronic systemic corticosteroids or other immunosuppressive/modulating agents (unless discontinued ≥1 week before first vaccine dose). Biomarkers required: IGHV unmutated status defined as less than 2% divergence from germline sequence, determined by molecular testing; provision of tumor (blood) and normal (saliva) specimens for whole‑exome and transcriptome sequencing. Biomarkers excluded: detection of del(17p) by fluorescent in‑situ hybridisation in ≥10% of CLL cells.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of severe allergic reaction attributable to any vaccine components; inability to receive tetanus booster vaccination; active, unmanaged autoimmune disease aside from controlled vitiligo, type 1 diabetes, stable thyroiditis, or mild psoriasis; uncontrolled intercurrent illness such as active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, significant cardiac arrhythmia; clinically significant hepatic dysfunction (AST/ALT >2.5 times upper limit of normal) or biliary obstruction; impaired renal function (creatinine clearance insufficient for safe dosing); known central nervous system/leptomeningeal involvement by CLL; active HIV infection, chronic hepatitis B or C infection; pregnancy or breastfeeding; inadequate performance status (ECOG ≥ 2); any condition judged by the investigator to jeopardise safety or data integrity.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Female participants who are pregnant or breastfeeding.

Corrected QT interval >470 ms, congenital Long QT syndrome, family history of prolonged QT or early sudden death.
Active lipodystrophy or lipoatrophy, or current use of drugs causing these conditions.
Unresolved active systemic bacterial, viral, or fungal infection requiring therapy.
Known HIV-positive status (patients with undetectable viral loads on HAART are permissible).
Chronic hepatitis B infection (managed according to guideline‐directed antiviral strategies) or active hepatitis C infection (treated and cleared patients allowable).
Past or recent (<6 months) chronic/recurrent pancreatitis or acute pancreatitis within 6 months.
Significant respiratory compromise (resting SpO₂ <92 % on ambient air or dependence on continuous supplementary oxygen).
Untreated or symptomatic central nervous system metastases, leptomeningeal disease, or carcinomatous meningitis (stable asymptomatic brain metastases allowed after ≥1 month stability post‑radiation).
Requirement for high‑dose glucocorticoids (>10 mg/day prednisone equivalents) (physiologic maintenance doses permitted if stable ≥1 month).
Anticipated need for prohibited concurrent therapy during the study.
Enrollment in any other investigative therapeutic trial concurrently.
Recent serious cardiovascular events within 6 months (unstabilized arrhythmias, significant ECG anomalies, recent stroke/TIA, myocardium injury, congestive heart failure class III/IV, recent pericarditis).
Severe hepatic, renal, hematologic, or metabolic derangements deemed unsafe by investigators.
Diagnosed type 1 diabetes mellitus or inadequately controlled type 2 diabetes mellitus (insulin users excluded; oral agents must be stable ≥14 days).
Hypersensitivity to FX‑909 or any formulation components.
Conditions impairing oral intake or intestinal absorption of tablets.
Immediate family relationship to study personnel at the enrolling institution without special IRB exemption.
Psychosocial, geographic, or logistical factors compromising adherence to protocol procedures.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Concurrent receipt of other investigative agents.
• Presence of active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.
• Allergic reaction to substances chemically or biochemically similar to dostarlimab or cobolimab, including Grade ≥ 3 hypersensitivity to monoclonal antibodies.
• Previous exposure to anti‑CTLA‑4, anti‑TIM3, or other experimental immune‑checkpoint inhibitors (aside from stipulated pembrolizumab use).
• History of interstitial lung disease.
• Active autoimmune disease that could exacerbate with immunostimulation (exceptions: well‑controlled hypothyroidism/euthyroidism, psoriasis, type I diabetes mellitus).
• Documented untreated Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection or Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome (eligibility only if HIV RNA suppressed, no AIDS‑defining opportunistic infections, and on ART ≥4 weeks).
• Active hepatitis B or C infection or uncompensated hepatic cirrhosis (exception: adequately suppressed HBV DNA on antiviral therapy).
• Systemic corticosteroid use exceeding physiological replacement (>5 mg prednisone daily equivalent) outside approved indications.
• Significant uncontrolled comorbidity—including unresolved infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, serious cardiac arrhythmia, or major psychiatric/sociomedical instability affecting protocol adherence.
• Clinically significant gastrointestinal pathology such as documented intestinal obstruction, persistent severe diarrhea, or active inflammatory bowel disease.
• Consumption of herbal supplements or folk remedy preparations (e.g., St. John’s wort, kava, ephedra, Ginkgo biloba, DHEA, yohimbé, saw palmetto, ginseng) during the study.
• Prior solid-organ or hematopoietic stem‑cell transplantation.
• Pregnancy or lactation.
• History of another malignant neoplasm unless disease‑free ≥5 years (certain cutaneous basaloid or in‑situ breast lesions acceptable within 5 years).

Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Uterine. Histology allowed: Serous carcinoma (any tumour containing a serous component; carcinosarcoma excluded). Cancer burden allowed: Recurrent or persistent disease with at least one measurable lesion according to RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: Minimum one prior platinum‑based chemotherapy regimen for advanced or metastatic disease (adjuvant platinum‑based chemotherapy counts if recurrence occurs within 12 months). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Tumour identified as microsatellite instability‑high or mismatch repair‑deficient AND documented prior exposure to a programmed death‑1/programmed death ligand‑1 immune checkpoint inhibitor (or determination that patient is not a candidate for such therapy). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of severe pulmonary toxicity or pneumonitis. Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including symptomatic congestive heart failure or left ventricular systolic impairment greater than NYHA Class IIIB. Significant hepatic insufficiency (bilirubin markedly elevated, transaminases > five times upper limit) or clinically meaningful renal impairment (creatinine clearance substantially reduced). Active untreated central nervous system metastasis or leptomeningeal disease. Ongoing infection with HIV, chronic hepatitis B or C with detectable viremia. Baseline Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status worse than 2. Concurrent malignant neoplasm judged to potentially confound safety or efficacy assessments. Pregnancy or lactation. Current use of potent cytochrome P450 3A4 enzyme modulators. Recent major surgical procedure (<28 days) or minor surgical procedure (<7 days). Dependence on enteral feeding tubes, total parental nutrition, or continuous intravenous fluids preventing safe oral administration. Chronic use of prohibited herbal products or dietary supplements. Any serious allergy to components related to the investigational product.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease unless adequately treated and steroid free for ≥14 days. Recent serious infection or ongoing antimicrobial therapy. Requirement for systemic immunosuppressive medication (>10 mg prednisolone equivalents daily). Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression. Preexisting moderate-to-severe lung disease or pneumonitis. History of grade 4 toxicity related to prior PD‑1 inhibition. Presence of another malignant neoplasm expected to require treatment within the next 24 months (except certain low‑risk skin cancers, carcinoma in situ of the cervix, localized prostate cancer). Active infection with Mycobacterium tuberculosis, human immunodeficiency virus, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C. Pregnancy or lactation. Significant cardiovascular disease (recent myocardial infarction ≤12 mo, unstable angina, congestive heart failure New York Heart Association class >III ≤6 mo). Poor functional status beyond Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score 2.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of monoclonal gammopathy of uncertain significance, smoldering myeloma, Waldenström’s macroglobulinaemia, or IgM myeloma; active plasma cell leukaemia; polyneuropathy, organomegaly, endocrinopathy, monoclonal protein and skin changes characteristic of POEMS syndrome; prior Stevens-Johnson syndrome; receipt of high‑dose corticosteroids concurrently (except chronic steroids prescribed for diseases other than myeloma); documented central nervous system involvement by multiple myeloma; active second malignancy aside from adequately treated basal cell carcinoma, cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma, in‑situ cervical cancer, stage I cancer in remission ≥2 years, or low‑risk prostate cancer (Gleason <7 and PSA <10 ng/mL); ongoing systemic infection requiring parenteral antimicrobial therapy; poorly controlled type 2 diabetes.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 3‑75 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: severe aplastic anemia. Histology allowed: acquired severe aplastic anemia with bone marrow cellularity < 25 % (hypocellular) and at least two of the following peripheral blood findings: neutrophil count < 0.5×10⁹/L, platelets < 20×10⁹/L, reticulocytes < 20×10⁹/L. Cancer burden allowed: NA. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: immunosuppressive therapy for severe aplastic anemia (anti‑thymocyte globulin, calcineurin inhibitors, thrombopoietin‑receptor agonists, androgens); prior allogeneic stem cell transplant; prior solid organ transplant; prior HLA‑desensitization interventions (plasmapheresis, ivIg, MMF, etc.); presence of donor‑specific anti‑HLA antibodies with mean fluorescent intensity > 3000. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: positive results for inherited bone‑marrow‑failure syndromes (e.g., abnormal Fanconi anemia diepoxybutane chromosomal breakage test or markedly reduced telomere length); clonal cytogenetic abnormalities characteristic of myelodysplastic syndrome (e.g., monosomy 7 or other WHO/ICC‑defined MDS lesions); donor‑specific anti‑HLA antibodies exceeding MFI 3000.
general_exclusion_criteria: Uncontrolled bacterial, viral, or fungal infection at enrolment; known life‑ threatening hypersensitivity reaction to rabbit anti‑Thymocyte Globulin; current pregnancy or lactation; prior malignancy requiring systemic therapy within five years (except adequately treated basal cell skin cancer or cervical intra‑epithelial neoplasia); active HIV infection with unsuppressed viral load or AIDS‑defining illness; chronic hepatitis B or C with detectable viral replication; inability to comply with mandated dual contraception measures; inadequate organ function outside predefined acceptable ranges (cardiac left ventricle ejection fraction ≤ 40 %; total bilirubin ≥ 2 mg/dL without Gilberts’ disease; creatinine clearance ≤ 60 mL/min for ages > 13 y or Glomerular Filtration Rate < 90 mL/min/1.73 m² for children < 13 y); performance status less than 60 % (Karnofsky/Lansky).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both males and females. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced or metastatic disease. general_exclusion_criteria:
Active or uncontrolled central nervous system metastases.
History of solid tumors other than the selected nectin‑4–expressing malignancies.
Cardiovascular events or conditions occurring within the preceding six months.
Pre‑existing peripheral neuropathy graded greater than or equal to Grade 2.
Hemoglobin A1c greater than or equal to 8 % or uncontrolled diabetes mellitus including existing diabetic neuropathy.
Active ocular disease at baseline.
Chronic severe liver disease or hepatic cirrhosis.
Interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis within six months before initiation of study treatment.
Other clinically significant comorbidities.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive carcinoma of the breast. Cancer burden allowed: anatomical stage I (Tumor ≤2 cm, classified as T1) with nodal status N0 or N1mi and no distant metastasis. Prior treatment required: definitive breast surgery (modified radical mastectomy or lumpectomy with sentinel node biopsy or axillary dissection) and radiation therapy to the conserved breast when breast-conserving surgery is performed. Prior treatment excluded: neoadjuvant or adjuvant chemotherapy for this breast cancer; prior hormonal therapy exceeding eight weeks for this breast cancer (aside from brief historical exposure >15 years ago); concomitant use of other investigational anticancer agents for breast cancer. Biomarkers required: HER2‑positive by ASCO/CAP 2018 guidelines **and** (Estrogen‑Receptor ≥10% **or** Progesterone‑Receptor ≥10%). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy, lactation, or lack of adequate non‑hormonal contraception for women of childbearing potential and men with partners of childpotential.; Prior invasive breast cancer.; Presence of locally advanced tumor features at initial presentation (e.g., fixation to chest wall, peau d’ orange, skin ulceration, inflammatory signs).; Uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses such as active systemic infection, end‑stage renal disease requiring dialysis, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable coronary artery disease, uncontrolled hypertension, or serious psychiatric disorders limiting compliance.; Significant hepatic impairment (AST/ALT > 5× upper limit of normal) or hyperbilirubinemia (>1.5 mg/dL).; Low hematopoietic reserve (ANC < 1000/mm³, platelet count < 75 000/mm³, hemoglobin < 8 g/dL).; Cardiac left ventricular ejection fraction < 50%;; Current participation in another therapeutic investigational drug study without sponsor permission.; Failure to comply with study procedures or inability to give informed consent.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Uncontrolled or symptomatic central nervous system metastases requiring urgent intervention.
Active or untreated brain/leptomeningeal disease.
Significant cardiovascular disease (heart failure NYHA class III‑IV, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within 6 months, serious arrhythmias).

Renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min) despite adequate hydration parameters outlined elsewhere.
Marked hepatic dysfunction (Child-Pugh class B/C, bilirubin >3×ULN unrelated to tumour).

Any condition compromising safety evaluation or compliance with protocol procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma resulting from Richter's transformation of chronic lymphocytic leukemia or small lymphocytic lymphoma. Histology allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: Presence of Richter's transformation (transformated aggressive lymphoma). Prior treatment required: None (patient may be newly diagnosed or previously treated). Prior treatment excluded: Any prior anti‑CD20 bispecific antibody. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of Hodgkin‐variant transformation of chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Prior receipt of polatuzumab vedotin (outside the designated combination arm) or prior atezolizumab (outside the designated combination arm). Recent (<14 days) investigational agents, targeted therapies, immune‑stimulating agents, cytokines, or monoclonal antibodies (excluding allowable continuation of BTK inhibition until day −1). Prior chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy within 30 days. Persistent Grade ≥1 toxicity from preceding anticancer therapy (exceptions noted). Bulky cervical adenopathy causing airway obstruction risk. Other concurrent malignant diagnoses except cured/chronic indolent forms (chronic lymphocytic/leukemic, non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, low‑risk prostate cancer on surveillance). Peripheral neuropathy worse than Grade 1 for polatuzumab recipients. History of serious immune‑related adverse events (Grade ≥3) aside from manageable endocrine toxicities. Confirmed progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy. Central nervous system involvement or leptomeningeal disease. Past or active interstitial/pneumonic infiltrates, idiopathic pneumonitis, or radiographic pneumonitis. Recurrent draining pleural, pericardial, or abdominal effusions. Solid organ transplantation. Suspected or known haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis. Active or poorly controlled autoimmune disease (specific exceptions outlined). Severe uncontrolled autoimmune cytopaenias needing recent escalation of therapy. Systemic immunosuppressants (other than stipulated steroids) within 2 weeks. Life‑ threatening allergies to monoclonal antibodies unamenable to desensitisation. Major surgery or trauma within 4 weeks with incomplete recovery. Positive HIV infection (unless controlled parameters met) or HTLV‑1 infection. Active cytomegalovirus infection. Clinically significant hepatic impairment, uncontrolled hepatitis B/C viremia, or cirrhosis. Ongoing serious bacterial/fungal/viral infection requiring inpatient care within 4 weeks. Live vaccine administration within 28 days or planned thereafter until B‑cell recovery. Cardiovascular events within 6 months (myocardial infarction, unstable angina, ACS, CABG/PTCA), NYHA class III/IV heart failure, left ventricular ejection fraction ≤40%, uncontrolled arrhythmias, prolonged QTc (>470 ms). Gastrointestinal malabsorption syndromes affecting drug uptake. Requirement for therapeutic anticoagulation with vitamin K antagonists. Hypersensitivity to study medication excipients. Previous major bleed or Grade ≥3 arterial thrombotic event on BTK inhibitor therapy. Bleeding diatheses (von Willebrand disease, hemophilia). Stroke or intracranial hemorrhage within 6 months. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intention to conceive during study participation. inability to comply with mandatory hospitalisations or procedural restraints.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Gastrointestinal stromal tumor. Histology allowed: Gastrointestinal stromal tumor. Cancer burden allowed: Inoperable or metastatic disease with at least one measurable lesion according to modified RECIST version 1.1. Prior treatment required: Prior receipt of imatinib with documented disease progression or intolerance **and** documented disease progression on sunitinib (dose ≥25 mg daily continuously or 37.5 mg daily on a 4‑weeks‑on/2‑weeks‑off schedule). Prior treatment excluded: Prior exposure to bezuclastinib. Biomarkers required: Primary KIT activating mutation identified in exon 11 or exon 9 (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: Primary KIT mutations other than exon 11 or exon 9; PDGFRA mutations; SDHx mutations; BRAF mutations; NF1 mutations; unknown genotype.
general_exclusion_criteria: Clinically significant cardiac disease or congestive heart failure greater than New York Heart Association class II; Baseline corrected QT interval exceeding 470 milliseconds; Recent acute coronary syndromes, myocardial infarction within 6 months, or unstable angina; Cerebrovascular accidents, deep vein thrombosis, or pulmonary embolism within 6 months (unless appropriately treated and resolved); Untreated central nervous system metastatic disease; Current pregnancy or lactation; Seropositivity for Human Immunodeficiency Virus; Severe uncontrolled concurrent medical illness compromising safety or protocol adherence; Major surgery (including abdominal laparotomy) within 4 weeks preceding study entry or insufficient recovery from surgery; Significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral medication absorption; Active uncontrolled bleeding (other than minor hemorrhoidal or gingival bleed); Unable to comply with study procedures; Active, uncontrolled systemic bacterial, fungal, or viral infection at screening.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: liver cancer (hepatocellular carcinoma). Histology allowed: hepatocellular carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: resectable disease with Child–Pugh class A liver function AND AJCC stage IA, IB, II, or IIIA **OR** BCLC stage 0 or A, measurable lesion per RECIST 1.1, no evidence of unresectable or metastatic spread. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic, locoregional, surgical, ablation, or radiofrequency/interventional therapy for hepatocellular carcinoma (patient must be treatment‐naïve). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of allogenic organ transplantation.; Receiving concomitant investigational agents.; Active or prior clinically significant autoimmune or inflammatory disorder (except selected mild dermatologic or endocrine conditions as detailed).; Documented severe allergy to components of durvalumab or tremelimumam.; Ongoing untreated active infection—including tuberculosis, uncontrolled bacterial/fungal infection.; Uncontrolled hypertension (>140/90 mmHg despite therapy).; Clinically evident interstitial lung disease or prior pneumonitis.; Significant cardiovascular disease (myocardial infarction or unstable angina within 12 months, NYHA class II or higher heart failure, uncontrolled arrhythmias.).; Coagulation abnormalities, active bleeding diatheses, or thrombotic events within 12 months.; Presence of primary central nervous system tumour (other than benign meningothelioma) or brain metastases, leptomeningeal disease, uncontrolled seizures.; Active primary immunodeficiency.; Positive serology indicating active hepatitis B infection (HBsAg positive) or active hepatitis C infection (detectable HCV RNA).; Pregnant or lactating individuals.; Requirement for total parenteral nutrition containing lipid emulsions.

Biomarkers required: KMT2A rearrangement or NPM1 mutation or NUP98 alteration or NUP214 alteration. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically significant pulmonary toxicity or pneumonitis.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including symptomatic heart failure or myocardial infarction within six months.
Severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh class C).
Severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 ml/min) not corrected by dialysis.
Active untreated or progressing intracranial malignancy or leptomeningeal carcinomatous involvement.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Women who are pregnant or breastfeeding.
Women of child‑bearing potential who do not use adequate contraceptive measures.
Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy within the past twelve months.
Recent exposure (within four weeks or five drug‑half‑lives) to systemic immunostimulatory agents (e.g., interferon, interleukin‑2).
Systemic glucocorticoid or other immunosuppressive medication (≥10 mg prednisolone daily or equivalent) within fourteen days prior to study drug administration or anticipated need thereafter.
History of grade ≥ 3 immune‑related adverse events such as non‑infectious pneumonitis, interstitial lung disease, or myocarditis.
Use of therapeutic oral or intravenous antibiotics within two weeks preceding study treatment.
Untreated or progressively worsening brain or central nervous system metastases (treated, steroid‑free, stable lesions acceptable).
Impaired cardiac function or significant underlying cardiac disease.
Uncontrolled hypercalcaemia (for Part D cohorts only).
Significant cardiovascular disease (New York Heart Association class II or higher, recent myocardial infarction, stroke, unstable arrhythmia, or unstable angina) within three months prior to study entry (Part D only).
Recent (<three months) hemolytic anemia or Evan’s syndrome.
Grade ≥ 3 peripheral neuropathy.
Concurrent therapeutic dosing of warfarin or other vitamin K antagonist anticoagulants.

History of progressive disease prior to enrolment.

Grade III or IV graft‑versus‑host disease.
Active hepatic graft‑vs‑host disease.
History of veno‑oclusive disease.

Active hepatitis B or C infection (positive serology with detectable viral DNA/RNA by PCR).

History of clinically significant pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.

Performance status worse than Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score 2 (or Karnofsky <50%).

Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic chemotherapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy, or radiation therapy directed at colorectal cancer apart from the optional single cycle described; prior invasive colon malignancy; prior bone marrow or solid organ transplantation; prior investigatorial anticancer therapy for colon cancer. Biomarkers required: Microsatellite stable tumour or intact mismatch repair proteins demonstrated by CLIA‑certified testing **and** circulating tumour DNA positive result obtained by Signatera™ assay. Biomarkers excluded: Microsatellite instability‑high tumour or deficient mismatch repair tumour **or** circulating tumour DNA negative result.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Active untreated cardiovascular disease or NYHA functional classification worse than class 2B.
Sensory or motor peripheral neuropathy grade ≥ 2.
Blood transfusion within 2 weeks preceding ctDNA blood draw.
Uncontrolled seizure disorder.
Active or chronic infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Known homozygous dihydropyrimidine dehydrogenase (DPD) deficiency.
Gilbert’s syndrome or homozygosity for UGT1A1*28 allele.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding at time of study entry or randomization.
Inability to tolerate planned duration of combination chemotherapy (≈6 months).
Significant concomitant medical or psychiatric illness rendering participation unsafe or interfering with assessments.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Smoldering multiple myeloma.
Non‑secretory multiple myeloma lacking measurable M‑protein or free light chains.
Active systemic AL amyloidosis.
Active plasma cell leukemia.
Recent (>14 days for arms 1‑11, >7 days for arm 12) red blood cell or platelet transfusions or blood growth factor support.
Radiation, chemotherapy, immunotherapy, or any tumor‑directed therapy ≤2 weeks prior to day 1 (radioimmunotherapy ≤6 weeks).
History of spinal cord compression with residual paralysis (dose escalation phase only).
Use of investigational anticancer agents ≤3 weeks before registration.
Autologous stem cell transplant <1 month ago or allogenic transplant <3 months ago.
Active graft‑versus‑host disease post‑allograft.
Life expectancy <3 months.
Major surgical procedure ≤4 weeks preceding enrolment.
Unstable cardiovascular disease—including symptomatic myocardial ischemia, uncontrolled arrhythmias, congestive heart failure NYHA class III‑IV, myocardial infarction <3 months, or left ventricular ejection fraction <50%.
Uncontrolled arterial hypertension.
Active infection necessitating intravenous antimicrobial therapy within 1 week.
Positive serology for hepatitis A, B, or C; known HIV infection.
Severe gastrointestinal disorders impairing tablet ingestion or drug absorption.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Serious ongoing psychiatric illness or medically significant condition interfering with compliance.
Hypersensitivity to any study medication components.
Baseline peripheral neuropathy grade > 2 or painful grade ≥ 2 (specified for SVd, SPVd, SNd arms).
Concurrent use of strong/moderate CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers when prohibited.
Previous exposure to a selective inhibitor of nuclear export (selinexor or other SINEs).
Corneal epithelial disease beyond mild punctate keratopathy (belantamab arm).
Any prior allogeneic stem cell or solid organ transplantation (sm d arm).
Known severe allergic reaction to thalidomide, lenalidomide, pomalidomide, carfilzomib, dexamethasone, CELMoD agents, or formulation excipients.
Refusal or inability to abstain from blood donation as mandated.
Unable or unwilling to undertake mandatory thrombosis prophylaxis.
Use of proton pump inhibitors within 2 weeks of initiation (when disallowed).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Bladder, ureter, renal pelvic, or urethral urothelial carcinoma. Histology allowed: Predominantly urothelial carcinoma (variant histology such as neuroendocrine differentiation, sarcomatoid, micropapillary, glandular, trophoblastic, Müllerian, or any degree of squamous differentiation tolerated provided tumour is not pure squamous cell carcinoma). Cancer burden allowed: Post‑radical cystectomy, nephroureterctomy, or ureterectomy with no gross residual disease; microscopically positive margins permitted; disease‑free status verified by RECIST 1.1 within 60 days; high‑risk pathological features per AJCC 7th edition defined as either (i) no prior neoadjuvant systemic therapy AND (pT3‑pT4* or pT0/x‑pT4 with N+ OR pT2N0 with central Signatera ctDNA⁺) OR (ii) receipt of neoadjuvant systemic therapy with resultant pathology ypT2‑ypT4a and/or ypN+. Prior treatment required: One of the following arms must be satisfied – (i) no neoadjuvant systemic therapy, no postoperative/adjuvant systemic therapy, no adjuvant radiation, and radical surgery performed 3‑12 weeks prior; OR (ii) receipt of neoadjuvant systemic therapy (cisplatin‑based chemotherapy alone or combined with PD‑1/PD‑L1 blockade, or enfortumab vedotin together with PD‑1/PD‑L1 blockade) administered before radical surgery. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior LAG‑3 blocking agent; prior investigational anticancer drug within four weeks of enrolment (time‑specific washouts omitted); prior systemic anticancer therapy inconsistent with selected arm (e.g., neoadjuvant therapy in arm i). Biomarkers required: Centralized Signatera circulating tumour DNA assay performed on surgically obtained tissue; ctDNA⁺ required for patients with pT2N0 tumours; ctDNA status may be positive or negative for all other enrolled cohorts. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: None

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive triple‑negative breast carcinoma. Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to checkpoint co‑stimulatory agents (e.g., CTLA‑4, OX‑40, CD137 agonists) **or** any HER2‑directed therapy **or** prior topoisomerase Ⅰ inhibitor‑containing regimens **or** antibody‑drug conjugates with a topoisomerase Ⅰ payload **or** prior endocrine therapy longer than four weeks **or** concurrent endocrine therapy during study treatment **or** documented recurrence of disease after neoadjuvant therapy and surgery. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor ≤10% **and** progesterone receptor ≤10% **and** HER2 negative according to ASCO/CAP guidelines. Biomarkers excluded: pathogenic germline BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutation.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of myocardial infarction or unstable angina within six months; history of serious ventricular arrhythmia, high‑grade AV block, or left ventricular ejection fraction less than 50%; active severe infections requiring antimicrobial therapy; inadequate organ function; uncontrolled central nervous system disease; HIV or chronic viral hepatitis infection; pregnant or breastfeeding persons; unable to give informed consent or comply with study procedures.

• Requirement for therapeutic anticoagulation.

• Retinal vein occlusion history.
• History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.

Active HIV infection with detectable viral load.
